questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-20 and IAS 33, when rights are issued at a price below fair value, there is a bonus element that requires an adjustment factor. The adjustment factor = Fair value per share before rights exercise / Theoretical ex-rights price = $25 / $23 = 1.0870 (rounded to 1.087). This factor is applied to shares outstanding before the rights issue to account for the bonus element, similar to a stock dividend adjustment. The theoretical ex-rights price = (4 × $25 + 1 × $15) / 5 = $115 / 5 = $23.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-55-20に基づき、公正価値未満の価格で新株引受権が発行される場合、ボーナス要素の調整係数が必要。調整係数 = 権利行使前の公正価値 ÷ 理論的権利落ち価格 = $25 ÷ $23 = 1.087。理論的権利落ち価格 = (4 × $25 + 1 × $15) ÷ 5 = $23。この係数を権利発行前の発行済株式数に乗じる。

questions.subtopic: EPS - Bonus Element in Rights Offering
questions.stem: Entity N has 500,000 shares outstanding on January 1. On July 1, it issues rights allowing shareholders to purchase 1 new share for every 4 shares held at an exercise price of $12 per share. The fair value of the stock immediately before the rights exercise is $20. All rights are exercised on July 1. What is the theoretical ex-rights price per share?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-20, the theoretical ex-rights price (TERP) is calculated as the weighted average of the fair value of existing shares and the exercise price of new shares. For every 4 existing shares at $20, 1 new share is issued at $12. TERP = (4 × $20 + 1 × $12) / (4 + 1) = ($80 + $12) / 5 = $92 / 5 = $18.40. The adjustment factor for pre-rights shares = Fair value before rights / TERP = $20 / $18.40 = 1.0870. This factor is used to restate pre-rights shares for EPS calculation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-55-20に基づき、理論的権利落ち価格(TERP)は、既存株式の公正価値と新株の行使価格の加重平均として計算される。既存4株（@$20）に対し新株1株（@$12）発行。TERP = (4 × $20 + 1 × $12) ÷ 5 = $92 ÷ 5 = $18.40。権利発行前株式の調整係数 = $20 ÷ $18.40 = 1.0870。

questions.stem: Entity O has 300,000 common shares outstanding and net income of $600,000. There are 20,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $15. The average market price during the year is $25. The options are the only potentially dilutive securities. What are the assumed proceeds used in the treasury stock method?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-29, the treasury stock method assumes that the proceeds from the exercise of options are used to repurchase shares at the average market price. Assumed proceeds = Number of options × Exercise price = 20,000 × $15 = $300,000. These proceeds are then assumed to repurchase shares: $300,000 / $25 average market price = 12,000 shares repurchased. Incremental shares = 20,000 − 12,000 = 8,000 shares added to the denominator for diluted EPS.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-29に基づき、自己株式法ではオプション行使による収入で平均市場価格で株式を買い戻すと仮定。想定収入 = 20,000 × $15 = $300,000。買戻し株数 = $300,000 ÷ $25 = 12,000株。増分株数 = 20,000 − 12,000 = 8,000株。

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Control Number (Income from Continuing Operations)
questions.stem: Entity P has income from continuing operations of $800,000, a loss from discontinued operations of $300,000, and net income of $500,000. There are 200,000 common shares outstanding and 10,000 convertible preferred shares (each convertible into 3 common shares) with annual dividends of $5 per share. Basic EPS from continuing operations is $3.75. When testing dilution, which amount is used as the 'control number'?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income of $500,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Income from continuing operations of $800,000, less preferred dividends of $50,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Income from continuing operations of $800,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss from discontinued operations of $300,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-19, the control number for determining whether potential common shares are dilutive is income from continuing operations available to common stockholders. This equals income from continuing operations ($800,000) less preferred dividends ($50,000 = 10,000 × $5) = $750,000. A security is dilutive only if its inclusion reduces EPS from continuing operations (the control number). Even if a security would increase EPS from net income, it cannot be included in diluted EPS if it is anti-dilutive with respect to the control number.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-19に基づき、潜在普通株式の希薄化判定に用いる「コントロールナンバー」は普通株主に帰属する継続事業からの利益。継続事業利益$800,000 − 優先配当$50,000 = $750,000。証券は継続事業EPSを低下させる場合のみ希薄化的。

questions.stem: Entity Q has net income of $400,000 and 100,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. Entity Q has 5,000 shares of 8%, $100 par, noncumulative preferred stock. No preferred dividends were declared during Year 1. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-11, for noncumulative preferred stock, only dividends that are actually declared during the period are deducted from net income to compute basic EPS. Since no preferred dividends were declared in Year 1, no deduction is made. Basic EPS = $400,000 / 100,000 = $4.00. If the preferred stock were cumulative, the dividend of $40,000 (5,000 × $100 × 8%) would be deducted regardless of whether declared.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-11に基づき、非累積優先株式の場合、実際に宣言された配当のみ純利益から控除する。Year 1に配当未宣言のため控除なし。基本EPS = $400,000 ÷ 100,000 = $4.00。累積優先株式であれば、宣言有無にかかわらず$40,000を控除する。

questions.stem: Entity R has net income of $1,200,000 and 500,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. The entity has $2,000,000 of 6% convertible bonds (each $1,000 bond convertible into 30 shares). The tax rate is 25%. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-40, the if-converted method is used for convertible bonds. Basic EPS = $1,200,000 / 500,000 = $2.40. For the diluted EPS numerator, add back interest expense net of tax: Interest = $2,000,000 × 6% = $120,000. Tax effect = $120,000 × 25% = $30,000. After-tax add-back = $120,000 − $30,000 = $90,000. Adjusted numerator = $1,200,000 + $90,000 = $1,290,000. For the denominator, add converted shares: 2,000 bonds × 30 shares = 60,000 additional shares. Adjusted denominator = 500,000 + 60,000 = 560,000. Diluted EPS = $1,290,000 / 560,000 = $2.30. Since $2.30 < $2.40 (basic EPS), the conversion is dilutive and diluted EPS is reported as $2.30.

questions.stem: Under legacy GAAP (prior to ASU 2020-06), Entity S issues a $1,000,000 convertible bond with a cash conversion feature. The fair value of a similar nonconvertible bond is $920,000. Under the legacy cash conversion model, what amount is allocated to the equity component?
questions.explanation: Under the legacy cash conversion model in ASC 470-20-25 (prior to ASU 2020-06), when a convertible bond contains a cash conversion feature, the instrument is separated into a liability component and an equity component. The liability component is measured at the fair value of a similar nonconvertible bond ($920,000). The equity component is the residual: $1,000,000 − $920,000 = $80,000. This equity component is credited to APIC. Note: ASU 2020-06 eliminated this separation requirement for most convertible instruments.
questions.explanationJa: 旧ASC 470-20-25（ASU 2020-06以前）のキャッシュ転換モデルでは、転換社債を負債要素と資本要素に分離する。負債要素 = 類似の非転換社債の公正価値$920,000。資本要素 = 残差$1,000,000 − $920,000 = $80,000。この資本要素はAPICに貸方計上。ASU 2020-06によりこの分離要件は大半の転換商品で廃止された。

questions.stem: Entity T enters into an accelerated share repurchase (ASR) agreement, paying $10,000,000 to an investment bank. On the initiation date, Entity T receives an initial delivery of 350,000 shares (based on the current stock price of $25). At settlement 3 months later, the volume-weighted average price (VWAP) was $20. How many total shares does Entity T receive?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 350,000 shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: 500,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 400,000 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 150,000 shares

questions.explanation: In an ASR agreement, the total number of shares is based on the VWAP during the calculation period. Total shares = Total payment / VWAP = $10,000,000 / $20 = 500,000 shares. Entity T initially received 350,000 shares, so at settlement it receives an additional 150,000 shares (500,000 − 350,000). Under ASC 505-30-25, the initial delivery is recorded as treasury stock, and the forward contract is classified in equity (indexed to own stock, physical settlement). At final settlement, the additional shares are added to treasury stock.
questions.explanationJa: ASR契約では、総株数は計算期間のVWAPに基づく。総株数 = $10,000,000 ÷ $20 = 500,000株。初回受領350,000株、決済時に追加150,000株を受領。ASC 505-30-25に基づき、初回受領は自己株式として計上、先渡契約は資本に分類。最終決済時に追加株式を自己株式に加算。

questions.stem: During Year 2, Entity U discovers that depreciation expense for Year 1 was understated by $60,000 due to a mathematical error. The tax rate is 30%. What is the net adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings at the beginning of Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $42,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $18,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase of $42,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-1, a correction of an error in previously issued financial statements is a prior period adjustment that requires retrospective restatement. The error caused depreciation to be understated by $60,000, meaning net income was overstated by $60,000 before tax. The tax effect = $60,000 × 30% = $18,000 (overstated tax expense would decrease). Net effect on retained earnings = $60,000 − $18,000 = $42,000 decrease. The beginning retained earnings for Year 2 is reduced by $42,000, net of tax.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-1に基づき、過年度の誤謬訂正は遡及的修正再表示が必要。減価償却費の過少計上$60,000により純利益が$60,000過大計上。税効果 = $60,000 × 30% = $18,000。利益剰余金への純影響 = $60,000 − $18,000 = $42,000の減少。Year 2期首の利益剰余金を税引後$42,000減額する。

questions.stem: Entity V has retained earnings of $800,000 and treasury stock (at cost) of $200,000. The state law restricts dividend payments to the extent of the cost of treasury stock held. What is the maximum amount available for dividends?
questions.explanation: Many states impose restrictions on dividend payments when treasury stock is held. Under ASC 505-30-50-2, entities must disclose restrictions on retained earnings due to treasury stock transactions. The restriction equals the cost of treasury stock ($200,000). Therefore, the unrestricted retained earnings available for dividends = $800,000 − $200,000 = $600,000. This restriction exists to protect creditors by ensuring the entity maintains sufficient capital.
questions.explanationJa: 多くの州法は自己株式保有時に配当支払いを制限する。ASC 505-30-50-2に基づき、自己株式取引による利益剰余金の制限を開示する必要がある。制限額 = 自己株式の取得原価$200,000。配当可能な非制限利益剰余金 = $800,000 − $200,000 = $600,000。

questions.stem: Parent P holds a 30% equity method investment in Subsidiary S with a carrying amount of $900,000. Parent P acquires an additional 40% of Subsidiary S for $1,400,000, obtaining control. On the acquisition date, the fair value of the previously held 30% interest is $1,050,000 and the fair value of the 30% noncontrolling interest is $1,050,000. The fair value of Subsidiary S's identifiable net assets is $3,000,000. What amount of goodwill is recognized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-10, in a step acquisition, the acquirer remeasures its previously held equity interest at acquisition-date fair value. Total consideration = Fair value of previously held interest ($1,050,000) + Acquisition-date payment ($1,400,000) + Fair value of NCI ($1,050,000) = $3,500,000. Goodwill = Total consideration − Fair value of identifiable net assets = $3,500,000 − $3,000,000 = $500,000. A gain of $150,000 ($1,050,000 − $900,000) is recognized on the remeasurement of the previously held interest.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-10に基づき、段階取得では既保有持分を取得日の公正価値で再測定する。対価合計 = 既保有持分の公正価値$1,050,000 + 追加取得$1,400,000 + NCIの公正価値$1,050,000 = $3,500,000。のれん = $3,500,000 − 識別可能純資産の公正価値$3,000,000 = $500,000。既保有持分の再測定で$150,000の利得を認識。

questions.stem: Parent P owns 80% of Subsidiary S. Subsidiary S declares a cash dividend of $100,000. What is the effect on the consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dividends expense of $100,000 is recognized in consolidated income

choices.label: B
choices.text: The NCI is reduced by $20,000 and no consolidated dividend expense is recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidated retained earnings is reduced by $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: The NCI is reduced by $100,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-21, when a subsidiary declares a dividend, the parent's share (80% × $100,000 = $80,000) is eliminated in consolidation as an intercompany transaction. The NCI's share (20% × $100,000 = $20,000) reduces the NCI balance in consolidated equity. No dividend expense is recognized in the consolidated income statement because dividends are distributions of equity, not expenses. The consolidated retained earnings is reduced only by the parent's share through the elimination process, and NCI equity is reduced by $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-21に基づき、子会社配当の親会社持分（80% × $100,000 = $80,000）は連結消去。NCIの持分（20% × $100,000 = $20,000）はNCI残高を減額。配当費用は連結損益計算書に認識されない。配当は資本の分配であり費用ではないため。

questions.stem: Parent P acquires 100% of Subsidiary S for $5,000,000. The book value of Subsidiary S's net assets is $3,500,000, and the fair value of identifiable net assets is $4,200,000. If push-down accounting is applied, what is the total stockholders' equity on Subsidiary S's separate balance sheet immediately after the acquisition?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-50-30-3, when push-down accounting is applied, the subsidiary's assets and liabilities are adjusted to their acquisition-date fair values on the subsidiary's own books. Additionally, goodwill is recognized on the subsidiary's books. Goodwill = $5,000,000 − $4,200,000 = $800,000. Total net assets pushed down = $4,200,000 + $800,000 = $5,000,000. The subsidiary's equity is reset to equal the total consideration paid by the parent, which is $5,000,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-50-30-3に基づき、プッシュダウン会計を適用すると、子会社の資産・負債は取得日の公正価値に調整される。のれん = $5,000,000 − $4,200,000 = $800,000。プッシュダウン後の純資産合計 = $4,200,000 + $800,000 = $5,000,000。子会社の株主資本は親会社が支払った対価と等しくなる。

questions.stem: Under SEC Staff Accounting Bulletin (SAB) Topic 4B, when a company has a deficit in retained earnings, what is the appropriate accounting treatment if it receives capital contributions from shareholders?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The contributions are recorded as revenue in the income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: The contributions are credited to Additional Paid-In Capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: The contributions are credited directly to Retained Earnings to offset the deficit

choices.label: D
choices.text: The contributions are recorded as deferred income on the balance sheet

questions.explanation: Under SEC SAB Topic 4B, capital contributions from shareholders are not revenue and should not be credited to retained earnings. Such contributions are credited to Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC). A deficit in retained earnings remains until offset by future earnings or eliminated through a quasi-reorganization. Shareholder contributions are equity transactions and are classified as contributed capital (APIC), not as income or a direct offset to accumulated deficit.
questions.explanationJa: SEC SAB Topic 4Bに基づき、株主からの資本拠出は収益ではなく、利益剰余金に直接計上すべきではない。資本拠出は追加払込資本(APIC)に貸方計上する。利益剰余金の欠損は将来の利益または準更生により解消されるまで残る。株主拠出は資本取引であり、APIC（拠出資本）に分類される。

questions.subtopic: Stock-Based Payment - Indexed to Own Stock
questions.stem: Entity W issues warrants to investors that have an exercise price that adjusts based on a stock price floor provision — if the stock price falls below $10, the exercise price automatically decreases to match the stock price. Under ASC 815-40, how should these warrants be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Equity, because they are settled in the entity's own shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: Liability, because the exercise price adjustment causes them to fail the indexation test

choices.label: C
choices.text: Equity, because the adjustment only applies in a limited scenario

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a derivative asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-40-15-7 and ASC 815-40-55-33 through 55-34, to be classified as equity, a freestanding financial instrument must be indexed to the entity's own stock. The indexation test requires analyzing the instrument's exercise contingencies and settlement provisions. A stock price floor provision that adjusts the exercise price based on the entity's stock price introduces a variable that is not solely based on the fixed-for-fixed criterion. Because the exercise price varies with the stock price, the instrument fails the indexation guidance and must be classified as a liability measured at fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815-40-15-7およびASC 815-40-55-33に基づき、資本分類には自社株式へのインデックス要件を満たす必要がある。株価フロア条項により行使価格が株価に連動して変動する場合、fixed-for-fixed基準を満たさず、インデックステストに不合格となるため負債に分類し、公正価値で測定する。

questions.stem: Entity X undergoes a recapitalization, exchanging its existing 100,000 shares of $10 par common stock for 200,000 shares of $5 par common stock. Before the recapitalization, APIC related to common stock was $500,000. What is APIC after the recapitalization?
questions.explanation: In a recapitalization where shares are exchanged for shares with a different par value but the same total par value is maintained, there is no change to APIC. Before: Common Stock = 100,000 × $10 = $1,000,000. After: Common Stock = 200,000 × $5 = $1,000,000. Since total par value remains $1,000,000 and no additional consideration is exchanged, APIC remains at $500,000. Total stockholders' equity is unchanged. This is economically similar to a 2-for-1 stock split effected as a stock exchange.
questions.explanationJa: 額面変更を伴う株式交換型の資本再構成で、総額面が変わらない場合、APICは変化しない。変更前：普通株式 = 100,000 × $10 = $1,000,000。変更後：普通株式 = 200,000 × $5 = $1,000,000。総額面$1,000,000が維持され追加対価がないため、APICは$500,000のまま。実質的に2対1の株式分割と同等。

questions.subtopic: EPS - Year-End vs Weighted Average Reconciliation
questions.stem: Entity Y had 200,000 shares outstanding on January 1, Year 1. On April 1, it issued 40,000 additional shares. On October 1, it repurchased 20,000 shares as treasury stock. Net income was $675,000 and no preferred stock is outstanding. What is basic EPS for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-10, basic EPS uses the weighted-average number of common shares outstanding during the period. Jan 1 – Mar 31 (3 months): 200,000 shares. Apr 1 – Sep 30 (6 months): 240,000 shares (200,000 + 40,000 issued). Oct 1 – Dec 31 (3 months): 220,000 shares (240,000 − 20,000 repurchased). Weighted average = (200,000 × 3/12) + (240,000 × 6/12) + (220,000 × 3/12) = 50,000 + 120,000 + 55,000 = 225,000 shares. Basic EPS = $675,000 / 225,000 = $3.00. Note that the year-end share count of 220,000 differs from the weighted average of 225,000 — EPS must always use the weighted average, not the year-end balance.

questions.stem: Marlin Corp. reacquires 3,000 shares of its $2 par common stock for $48 per share, using the cost method. Which statement correctly describes the effect of this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity decreases by $144,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity decreases by $6,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Common stock decreases by $6,000 and APIC decreases by $138,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no effect on total stockholders' equity

questions.explanation: Under the cost method (ASC 505-30-30-7), treasury stock is debited at its full reacquisition cost of 3,000 × $48 = $144,000, with a credit to Cash for the same amount. Treasury stock is a contra-equity account, so total stockholders' equity decreases by the full $144,000. Par value and APIC are not affected at acquisition under the cost method; that allocation occurs only under the par value method.
questions.explanationJa: 原価法（ASC 505-30-30-7）では、自己株式は再取得原価3,000 × $48 = $144,000全額で借方計上し、同額を現金で貸方計上する。自己株式は資本の控除勘定であるため、株主資本合計は$144,000減少する。取得時には額面とAPICには影響しない（その配分は額面法でのみ行われる）。

questions.stem: Tarpon Inc. holds 4,000 shares of treasury stock acquired at $42 per share under the cost method. It reissues 2,500 of those shares at $50 per share. What amount is credited to additional paid-in capital from treasury stock transactions?
questions.explanation: Under the cost method, treasury stock reissued above cost generates additional paid-in capital. Cash received = 2,500 × $50 = $125,000. Treasury stock is credited at cost = 2,500 × $42 = $105,000. The excess of proceeds over cost, $125,000 − $105,000 = $20,000, is credited to additional paid-in capital from treasury stock. No gain is recognized in net income on a company's own equity transactions.
questions.explanationJa: 原価法では、原価超で再発行した自己株式は資本剰余金を生む。受取現金 = 2,500 × $50 = $125,000。自己株式は原価で貸方計上 = 2,500 × $42 = $105,000。手取金が原価を超える額$125,000 − $105,000 = $20,000を資本剰余金（自己株式取引）に貸方計上する。自社の資本取引で純利益に利得は認識しない。

questions.stem: Bonita Co. has 600,000 shares of $4 par common stock issued and outstanding and additional paid-in capital of $9,000,000. The board declares a 3-for-1 forward stock split. Immediately after the split, what are the par value per share and the additional paid-in capital balance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Par value $12.00 per share; APIC $9,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Par value $4.00 per share; APIC $27,000,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Par value $1.333 per share; APIC $3,000,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Par value $1.333 per share; APIC $9,000,000

questions.explanation: A stock split changes the par value per share and the number of shares but requires only a memorandum entry — no dollar amounts in equity accounts change. After a 3-for-1 split, shares triple to 1,800,000 and par value is divided by 3: $4 / 3 = $1.333 per share. Total par remains 1,800,000 × $1.333 = $2,400,000 (unchanged from 600,000 × $4). Additional paid-in capital is unaffected and remains $9,000,000.
questions.explanationJa: 株式分割は1株あたり額面と株式数を変更するが、備忘記録のみで資本勘定の金額は変わらない。3対1分割後、株式数は3倍の1,800,000株となり、額面は3で除して$4 / 3 = $1.333となる。額面総額は1,800,000 × $1.333 = $2,400,000で不変。資本剰余金は影響を受けず$9,000,000のまま。

questions.subtopic: Small Stock Dividend - Retained Earnings Effect
questions.stem: Sailfish Corp. has 80,000 shares of $1 par common stock outstanding with a current market price of $36 per share. The board declares a 15% stock dividend. What is the reduction in retained earnings resulting from the dividend?
questions.explanation: A stock dividend below the 20–25% threshold is a small stock dividend recorded at fair market value (ASC 505-20-30-3). New shares issued = 80,000 × 15% = 12,000. Retained earnings is reduced by the fair value of those shares = 12,000 × $36 = $432,000. Of that, $12,000 (12,000 × $1 par) is credited to common stock and $420,000 to additional paid-in capital.
questions.explanationJa: 20〜25%未満の株式配当は小規模株式配当であり、公正市場価値で記録する（ASC 505-20-30-3）。新規発行株式 = 80,000 × 15% = 12,000株。利益剰余金はその公正価値分減少する = 12,000 × $36 = $432,000。うち$12,000（12,000 × $1額面）を普通株式、$420,000を資本剰余金に貸方計上する。

questions.subtopic: Basic EPS - Cumulative Preferred and Mid-Year Issuance
questions.stem: Halibut Corp. reported net income of $945,000 for the year. It has 8,000 shares of 7%, $50 par cumulative preferred stock outstanding all year. Common shares were 300,000 on January 1, and 60,000 additional common shares were issued on May 1. What is basic EPS for the year?
questions.explanation: Cumulative preferred dividends are deducted whether or not declared: 8,000 × $50 × 7% = $28,000. Income available to common = $945,000 − $28,000 = $917,000. Weighted-average shares = 300,000 × 12/12 + 60,000 × 8/12 = 300,000 + 40,000 = 340,000. Basic EPS = $917,000 / 340,000 = $2.70.

questions.stem: Pompano Corp. has net income of $1,200,000 and 400,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. It has 50,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $20; the average market price of the stock during the year was $32. The options are the only potentially dilutive security. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Applying the treasury stock method (ASC 260-10-45-23): assumed proceeds = 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000; shares repurchased at the average market price = $1,000,000 / $32 = 31,250; incremental dilutive shares = 50,000 − 31,250 = 18,750. Diluted shares = 400,000 + 18,750 = 418,750. Diluted EPS = $1,200,000 / 418,750 = $2.87.

questions.subtopic: Stock Compensation - Graded Vesting Accelerated Method
questions.stem: Wahoo Inc. grants 6,000 stock options with graded vesting: 2,000 vest at the end of Year 1, 2,000 at the end of Year 2, and 2,000 at the end of Year 3. The total grant-date fair value is $120,000 ($20 per option). Using the graded-vesting (accelerated) attribution method, what compensation expense is recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the accelerated method (ASC 718-10-35-8), each tranche is treated as a separate award amortized over its own service period. Each tranche has fair value 2,000 × $20 = $40,000. Year 1 expense = $40,000 × 1/1 (first tranche) + $40,000 × 1/2 (second tranche) + $40,000 × 1/3 (third tranche) = $40,000 + $20,000 + $13,333 = $73,333.

questions.stem: Snapper Co. grants 2,000 cash-settled stock appreciation rights (SARs) that vest over a three-year service period. The fair value of each SAR is $12 at the end of Year 1 and $18 at the end of Year 2. What is the cumulative compensation liability recognized at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are liability-classified and remeasured to fair value each period (ASC 718-30-35-3). The cumulative liability equals total fair value × the cumulative portion of the service period elapsed. At the end of Year 2: 2,000 × $18 × 2/3 = $24,000. The Year 1 liability was 2,000 × $12 × 1/3 = $8,000, so the Year 2 incremental expense is $16,000, but the cumulative recognized liability at the end of Year 2 is $24,000.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済のSARは負債分類であり、各期に公正価値で再測定する（ASC 718-30-35-3）。累積負債 = 総公正価値 × 経過した勤務期間の累積割合。Year 2末：2,000 × $18 × 2/3 = $24,000。Year 1負債は2,000 × $12 × 1/3 = $8,000であったため、Year 2の増分費用は$16,000だが、Year 2末の累積負債は$24,000である。

questions.stem: Grouper Corp. has total stockholders' equity of $8,000,000. Its preferred stock consists of 30,000 shares of $50 par, 5% cumulative preferred, callable at $52 per share, with one year of dividends in arrears. There are 400,000 common shares outstanding. What is the book value per common share?
questions.explanation: The preferred claim equals the call (liquidation) value plus cumulative dividends in arrears. Call value = 30,000 × $52 = $1,560,000. Dividends in arrears = 30,000 × $50 × 5% × 1 year = $75,000. Total preferred claim = $1,635,000. Equity available to common = $8,000,000 − $1,635,000 = $6,365,000. Book value per common share = $6,365,000 / 400,000 = $15.91.

questions.stem: Dorado Corp. has net income of $800,000 and 250,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. It also has 20,000 shares of convertible preferred stock paying an annual dividend of $3 per share; each preferred share is convertible into 2 common shares. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Basic EPS = ($800,000 − $60,000 preferred dividends) / 250,000 = $740,000 / 250,000 = $2.96. Under the if-converted method (ASC 260-10-45-40), the preferred dividend of $60,000 (20,000 × $3) is added back to the numerator and the 40,000 conversion shares (20,000 × 2) are added to the denominator: $800,000 / 290,000 = $2.76. Because $2.76 is below basic EPS of $2.96, the conversion is dilutive and diluted EPS is $2.76.

questions.stem: Mackerel Corp. has net income of $2,100,000 and 800,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. It has $3,000,000 of 5% convertible bonds outstanding; each $1,000 bond is convertible into 25 common shares. The effective tax rate is 21%. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Basic EPS = $2,100,000 / 800,000 = $2.63. Under the if-converted method, the after-tax interest is added back to the numerator: interest = $3,000,000 × 5% = $150,000; after tax = $150,000 × (1 − 0.21) = $118,500; adjusted numerator = $2,218,500. Conversion shares = (3,000 bonds × 25) = 75,000; adjusted denominator = 875,000. Diluted EPS = $2,218,500 / 875,000 = $2.54. Because $2.54 is below basic EPS, the bonds are dilutive.

questions.stem: Barracuda Co. has 200,000 shares of $2 par common stock outstanding with a market price of $30 per share. It declares a 50% stock dividend. At what amount is retained earnings reduced to record this dividend?
questions.explanation: A stock dividend of 20–25% or more is a large stock dividend recorded at par value, not market value (ASC 505-20-30-3). Shares issued = 200,000 × 50% = 100,000. Retained earnings is reduced by 100,000 × $2 par = $200,000, with an equal credit to common stock. No additional paid-in capital arises on a large stock dividend recorded at par.
questions.explanationJa: 20〜25%以上の株式配当は大型株式配当であり、時価ではなく額面価額で記録する（ASC 505-20-30-3）。発行株式 = 200,000 × 50% = 100,000株。利益剰余金は100,000 × $2額面 = $200,000減少し、同額を普通株式に貸方計上する。額面で記録する大型株式配当では資本剰余金は生じない。

questions.subtopic: Weighted Average Shares - Split After Issuance
questions.stem: Albacore Corp. had 120,000 common shares outstanding on January 1. On April 1, it issued 30,000 additional shares for cash. On August 1, it declared a 2-for-1 stock split. What is the weighted-average number of shares used in the basic EPS computation for the year?
questions.explanation: Stock splits are applied retroactively to the beginning of the period for all shares already issued, including the April 1 issuance (ASC 260-10-55-12). Restated shares: 120,000 × 2 = 240,000 from January 1, and 30,000 × 2 = 60,000 issued April 1. January 1 – March 31: 240,000 × 3/12 = 60,000. April 1 – December 31: 300,000 × 9/12 = 225,000. Weighted average = 60,000 + 225,000 = 285,000.

questions.stem: Sturgeon Inc. grants 4,000 shares of restricted stock to an executive on January 1, Year 1. The shares vest after a four-year service period and are settled in shares. The grant-date fair value is $25 per share; on the vesting date the share price has risen to $40. Assuming no forfeitures, what total compensation cost does Sturgeon recognize over the service period?
questions.explanation: Share-settled restricted stock is an equity-classified award measured at grant-date fair value and not remeasured (ASC 718-10-30-2). Total compensation cost = 4,000 × $25 = $100,000, recognized straight-line over the four-year service period ($25,000 per year). The rise in share price to $40 on the vesting date does not change the expense because equity-classified awards are locked to grant-date fair value.
questions.explanationJa: 株式決済の譲渡制限付株式は持分分類のアワードであり、付与日公正価値で測定し再測定しない（ASC 718-10-30-2）。報酬費用総額 = 4,000 × $25 = $100,000を4年の勤務期間にわたり定額で認識する（年$25,000）。権利確定日の株価$40への上昇は費用に影響しない。持分分類のアワードは付与日公正価値に固定されるためである。

questions.stem: Anchovy Corp. holds 3,000 treasury shares acquired at $48 per share under the cost method. The balance in additional paid-in capital from treasury stock is $5,000. Anchovy reissues 1,500 of the shares at $40 per share. What amount is debited to retained earnings on this reissuance?
questions.explanation: Cash received = 1,500 × $40 = $60,000; treasury stock is credited at cost = 1,500 × $48 = $72,000, leaving a $12,000 shortfall. Under the cost method (ASC 505-30-30-8), the shortfall is first absorbed by existing additional paid-in capital from treasury stock ($5,000), and any remainder is debited to retained earnings. Retained earnings is debited for $12,000 − $5,000 = $7,000.
questions.explanationJa: 受取現金 = 1,500 × $40 = $60,000。自己株式は原価で貸方計上 = 1,500 × $48 = $72,000となり、$12,000の不足が生じる。原価法（ASC 505-30-30-8）では、不足額はまず既存の資本剰余金（自己株式）$5,000で吸収し、残額を利益剰余金から借方計上する。利益剰余金は$12,000 − $5,000 = $7,000借方計上される。

questions.stem: Which of the following items would be reported in accumulated other comprehensive income rather than in net income under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Realized gain on the sale of equipment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unrealized holding gain on a trading debt security

choices.label: C
choices.text: Effective portion of the gain on a cash flow hedging derivative

choices.label: D
choices.text: Unrealized holding gain on an equity security with a readily determinable fair value

questions.explanation: The effective portion of the gain or loss on a derivative designated as a cash flow hedge is deferred in other comprehensive income and accumulated in AOCI until the hedged transaction affects earnings (ASC 220-10-45-10A). Realized equipment gains and unrealized gains on trading securities go directly to net income, and under ASC 321 unrealized gains on equity securities with readily determinable fair values are also recognized in net income.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュフローヘッジに指定されたデリバティブの損益の有効部分は、ヘッジ対象取引が損益に影響するまでその他の包括利益に繰り延べAOCIに累積する（ASC 220-10-45-10A）。設備売却益や売買目的証券の未実現益は純利益に直接計上され、ASC 321により公正価値が容易に算定できる持分証券の未実現益も純利益に認識される。

questions.stem: Permit Corp. acquires 75% of Bonefish Corp. for $6,000,000 cash. The fair value of Bonefish's identifiable net assets is $6,400,000, and the fair value of the 25% noncontrolling interest is $1,900,000. Under ASC 805, what amount of goodwill is recognized in the consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: U.S. GAAP requires the full (fair-value) goodwill method. Goodwill = (consideration transferred + fair value of the noncontrolling interest) − fair value of identifiable net assets = ($6,000,000 + $1,900,000) − $6,400,000 = $1,500,000 (ASC 805-20-30-1). This total includes goodwill attributable to both the controlling and noncontrolling interests.
questions.explanationJa: U.S. GAAPは全部（公正価値）のれん法を求める。のれん = （移転対価 + 非支配持分の公正価値）− 識別可能純資産の公正価値 = ($6,000,000 + $1,900,000) − $6,400,000 = $1,500,000（ASC 805-20-30-1）。この総額には支配持分と非支配持分の双方に帰属するのれんが含まれる。

questions.subtopic: Fully Participating Preferred Stock - Dividend Allocation
questions.stem: Crevalle Corp. has 8,000 shares of $100 par, 5% fully participating preferred stock and 40,000 shares of $10 par common stock outstanding. The board declares total dividends of $150,000. How much is allocated to the preferred shareholders?
questions.explanation: Preferred par = 8,000 × $100 = $800,000; common par = 40,000 × $10 = $400,000. Step 1 — preferred current dividend: $800,000 × 5% = $40,000. Step 2 — matching common dividend: $400,000 × 5% = $20,000. Step 3 — remainder $150,000 − $40,000 − $20,000 = $90,000 is shared on relative par; preferred share = $90,000 × ($800,000 / $1,200,000) = $60,000. Preferred total = $40,000 + $60,000 = $100,000.

questions.stem: Ladyfish Corp. carries out a quasi-reorganization to eliminate an accumulated deficit of $3,000,000. Immediately before the readjustment, additional paid-in capital is $4,500,000 and the asset write-downs have already been recorded. After the quasi-reorganization, what are the balances of retained earnings and additional paid-in capital?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retained earnings $1,500,000; APIC $0

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retained earnings $0; APIC $1,500,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings $(3,000,000); APIC $4,500,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retained earnings $0; APIC $4,500,000

questions.explanation: In a quasi-reorganization, the accumulated deficit is eliminated against additional paid-in capital (ASC 852-20-25). The $3,000,000 deficit is charged against APIC, reducing it from $4,500,000 to $1,500,000, and retained earnings is reset to $0. The newly dated retained earnings then begins to accumulate from a zero balance, and the date of the readjustment is disclosed for a period of time.
questions.explanationJa: 準更生では、累積欠損金を資本剰余金に充当して消去する（ASC 852-20-25）。欠損金$3,000,000をAPICから控除し、$4,500,000から$1,500,000に減少させ、利益剰余金を$0にリセットする。日付を付した利益剰余金はゼロから累積を開始し、再調整の日付は一定期間開示される。

questions.stem: Drum Corp. sponsors an employee stock purchase plan open to substantially all full-time employees, with no option features other than a short enrollment period. For the plan to qualify as noncompensatory under ASC 718, the purchase discount from market price generally must not exceed which threshold?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-50-25-1, an ESPP is noncompensatory only if it meets all specified conditions, including that the discount from market price does not exceed the per-share issuance cost the entity would otherwise incur — a discount of 5% or less is the safe harbor presumed not to exceed those costs. A discount above 5% makes the plan compensatory unless the entity can justify a higher percentage based on actual avoided issuance costs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-50-25-1により、ESPPが非報酬型となるのは、市場価格からの割引が企業が本来負担する1株当たり発行コストを超えないこと等の所定要件をすべて満たす場合のみである。5%以下の割引が当該コストを超えないと推定される安全基準（セーフハーバー）である。5%超の割引は、回避された発行コストに基づき高い割引率を正当化できない限り報酬型となる。

questions.subtopic: Cumulative Preferred - Dividends in Arrears Allocation
questions.stem: Tilapia Corp. has 12,000 shares of 6%, $100 par cumulative preferred stock outstanding. No dividends were declared in Year 1 or Year 2. In Year 3 the board declares total dividends of $300,000. How much of the Year 3 dividend is allocated to the preferred shareholders?
questions.explanation: The annual preferred entitlement is 12,000 × $100 × 6% = $72,000. Because the stock is cumulative, the two prior years in arrears ($72,000 × 2 = $144,000) plus the Year 3 current entitlement ($72,000) must be paid before any common dividend. Preferred total = $144,000 + $72,000 = $216,000. Since $300,000 exceeds $216,000, preferred receives $216,000 and common receives the remaining $84,000.
questions.explanationJa: 優先株の年間配当は12,000 × $100 × 6% = $72,000。累積的であるため、過去2年の未払分（$72,000 × 2 = $144,000）に当期Year 3分（$72,000）を加えた額を普通株配当に先立ち支払う必要がある。優先株合計 = $144,000 + $72,000 = $216,000。$300,000が$216,000を超えるため、優先株に$216,000、普通株に残り$84,000が配分される。

questions.stem: Pickerel Corp. has 6,000 shares of $25 par callable preferred stock outstanding, originally issued at $28 per share. The stock is callable at $31 per share, and Pickerel calls and retires all of the shares. Which journal entry correctly records the retirement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $150,000; Debit APIC—Preferred $36,000; Credit Cash $186,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $150,000; Debit APIC—Preferred $18,000; Debit Retained Earnings $18,000; Credit Cash $186,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $186,000; Credit Cash $186,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $150,000; Credit APIC—Preferred $36,000; Credit Cash $186,000

questions.explanation: On retirement, preferred stock is removed at par (6,000 × $25 = $150,000) and the related APIC from original issuance is removed (6,000 × ($28 − $25) = $18,000). The call price exceeds the original issue price by $3 per share, so 6,000 × ($31 − $28) = $18,000 is charged to retained earnings (an excess of reacquisition cost over carrying amount is not a gain and never increases income). Cash paid = 6,000 × $31 = $186,000.
questions.explanationJa: 退却時、優先株式を額面で除去（6,000 × $25 = $150,000）し、当初発行時の関連APICを除去する（6,000 × ($28 − $25) = $18,000）。償還価格が当初発行価格を1株$3超えるため、6,000 × ($31 − $28) = $18,000を利益剰余金に賦課する（再取得原価が帳簿価額を超える額は利得ではなく、純利益を増加させない）。支払現金 = 6,000 × $31 = $186,000。

questions.stem: During Year 2, Croaker Corp. discovers that ending inventory at the end of Year 1 was overstated by $80,000 due to a counting error. The applicable tax rate is 25%. What is the effect on the opening balance of retained earnings at the beginning of Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $20,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $60,000

questions.explanation: An overstatement of ending inventory in Year 1 understated cost of goods sold and therefore overstated pre-tax income by $80,000. Correcting the error retrospectively (ASC 250-10-45-23) reduces beginning retained earnings of Year 2 by the after-tax amount: $80,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $60,000. The error correction is a prior period adjustment to opening retained earnings, not an item of current-year income.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1期末棚卸資産の過大計上は売上原価を過少にし、税引前利益を$80,000過大にした。誤謬を遡及修正する（ASC 250-10-45-23）と、Year 2期首利益剰余金は税引後額だけ減少する：$80,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $60,000。誤謬訂正は期首利益剰余金への前期損益修正であり、当期利益の項目ではない。

questions.stem: Mullet Corp. receives subscriptions for 8,000 shares of $5 par common stock at $18 per share, collecting a 30% down payment in cash. Which journal entry correctly records the subscription?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $43,200; Credit Common Stock $40,000; Credit APIC $3,200

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $144,000; Credit Common Stock Subscribed $40,000; Credit APIC $104,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $43,200; Debit Stock Subscriptions Receivable $100,800; Credit Common Stock Subscribed $40,000; Credit APIC $104,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Cash $43,200; Debit Stock Subscriptions Receivable $100,800; Credit Common Stock $144,000

questions.explanation: Total subscription price = 8,000 × $18 = $144,000. Cash down payment = 30% × $144,000 = $43,200; stock subscriptions receivable = $100,800. Common stock subscribed is credited at par = 8,000 × $5 = $40,000, and APIC is credited for the excess over par = 8,000 × ($18 − $5) = $104,000. The shares are formally issued only when the subscription is fully collected; until then, common stock subscribed is reported within equity.
questions.explanationJa: 引受価額合計 = 8,000 × $18 = $144,000。現金頭金 = 30% × $144,000 = $43,200、引受金未収金 = $100,800。普通株式引受済を額面で貸方計上 = 8,000 × $5 = $40,000、APICを額面超過分で貸方計上 = 8,000 × ($18 − $5) = $104,000。株式は引受金が全額回収された時点で正式発行され、それまでは普通株式引受済を資本に表示する。

questions.stem: Pike Corp. declares a property dividend consisting of investment securities to be distributed to common shareholders. On the declaration date the securities have a carrying amount of $180,000 and a fair value of $240,000. What gain (if any) does Pike recognize on the declaration date, and at what amount is the dividend liability recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No gain; dividend liability of $180,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $60,000; dividend liability of $180,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No gain; dividend liability of $240,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $60,000; dividend liability of $240,000

questions.explanation: A nonreciprocal transfer of nonmonetary assets to owners (a property dividend) is measured at the fair value of the assets distributed (ASC 845-10-30-1). On the declaration date the securities are first remeasured to fair value, producing a gain of $240,000 − $180,000 = $60,000 in net income, and the dividend liability and the charge to retained earnings are both recorded at the $240,000 fair value.
questions.explanationJa: 所有者への非貨幣性資産の非互恵的移転（財産配当）は、分配される資産の公正価値で測定する（ASC 845-10-30-1）。宣言日にまず証券を公正価値に再測定し、$240,000 − $180,000 = $60,000の利得を純利益に計上する。配当負債および利益剰余金への賦課はいずれも公正価値$240,000で記録する。

questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Carp Corp. declares a scrip dividend by issuing notes totaling $200,000 to shareholders, payable on April 1, Year 2, and bearing interest at 8% per annum. What is the total amount Carp will pay shareholders on April 1, Year 2?
questions.explanation: A scrip dividend is a promissory note to pay a dividend at a later date, generally with interest. The $200,000 principal is charged to retained earnings on the declaration date. Interest accrues for six months (October 1 to April 1): $200,000 × 8% × 6/12 = $8,000, recognized as interest expense, not as a dividend. Total cash paid at maturity = $200,000 + $8,000 = $208,000.
questions.explanationJa: 手形配当は将来の期日に配当を支払う約束手形で、通常利息が付く。元本$200,000は宣言日に利益剰余金へ賦課する。利息は6か月（10月1日〜4月1日）発生する：$200,000 × 8% × 6/12 = $8,000で、配当ではなく利息費用として認識する。満期の支払総額 = $200,000 + $8,000 = $208,000。

questions.stem: Bluegill Corp. has unrecognized prior service cost of $150,000 in accumulated other comprehensive income related to a defined benefit pension plan amendment. The average remaining service period of active participants is 12 years. Using straight-line amortization, what amount is reclassified from AOCI into net periodic pension cost in the first year?
questions.explanation: Prior service cost arising from a plan amendment is initially recorded in other comprehensive income and then amortized into net periodic pension cost over the average remaining service period of the active employees expected to receive benefits (ASC 715-30-35-11). Straight-line amortization = $150,000 / 12 years = $12,500 per year, which is reclassified out of AOCI and reduces the AOCI balance by $12,500 in the first year.
questions.explanationJa: 制度改定により生じた過去勤務費用は、まずその他の包括利益に計上し、給付を受けると見込まれる現役従業員の平均残存勤務期間にわたって純年金費用に償却する（ASC 715-30-35-11）。定額償却 = $150,000 / 12年 = $12,500/年で、これがAOCIから組み替えられ、初年度にAOCI残高を$12,500減少させる。

questions.subtopic: Stock Issuance Costs - Effect on APIC
questions.stem: Perch Corp. issues 20,000 shares of $1 par common stock at $25 per share and incurs $40,000 of direct, incremental issuance costs (legal, underwriting, and registration fees). After recording the issuance, what is the balance of additional paid-in capital attributable to this transaction?
questions.explanation: Direct, incremental costs of issuing equity are charged against the proceeds and reduce additional paid-in capital; they are not expensed (ASC 505-10-25-2; SEC SAB Topic 5A). Gross proceeds = 20,000 × $25 = $500,000. Common stock is credited at par = 20,000 × $1 = $20,000, leaving $480,000 of APIC before costs. Subtracting the $40,000 of issuance costs gives APIC = $480,000 − $40,000 = $440,000.
questions.explanationJa: 株式発行の直接増分コストは発行収入から控除し資本剰余金を減額する。費用計上はしない（ASC 505-10-25-2、SEC SAB Topic 5A）。総収入 = 20,000 × $25 = $500,000。普通株式を額面で貸方計上 = 20,000 × $1 = $20,000で、コスト控除前のAPICは$480,000。発行コスト$40,000を差し引くとAPIC = $480,000 − $40,000 = $440,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Sole Corp. grants 20,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $6 per option and a four-year cliff vesting period. The initial estimated forfeiture rate is 8%. At the end of Year 3, management revises the estimated forfeiture rate to 12%. What is the cumulative compensation expense recognized through the end of Year 3?
questions.explanation: Compensation cost is recognized based on the awards expected to vest, with forfeiture estimates revised as a change in estimate using a cumulative catch-up (ASC 718-10-35-3). At the end of Year 3, options expected to vest = 20,000 × (1 − 0.12) = 17,600; total expected compensation = 17,600 × $6 = $105,600. Recognized over four years, the cumulative amount through Year 3 = $105,600 × 3/4 = $79,200.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬費用は権利確定見込みのアワードに基づき認識し、失効率の見積変更は累積的キャッチアップにより処理する（ASC 718-10-35-3）。Year 3末、権利確定見込みのオプション = 20,000 × (1 − 0.12) = 17,600個、見込報酬総額 = 17,600 × $6 = $105,600。4年で認識するため、Year 3末までの累計 = $105,600 × 3/4 = $79,200。

questions.subtopic: Noncontrolling Interest - Allocation of Subsidiary Income
questions.stem: Flounder Corp. owns 70% of Sole Inc., which reports net income of $500,000 for the year with no intercompany transactions. In the consolidated statement of operations, what amount of net income is allocated to the noncontrolling interest?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-19, consolidated net income is allocated between the controlling and noncontrolling interests based on their ownership percentages. The noncontrolling interest's share = 30% × $500,000 = $150,000. This amount is presented as net income attributable to the noncontrolling interest on the face of the consolidated income statement, and the controlling interest is allocated the remaining $350,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-19により、連結純利益は持分比率に基づき支配持分と非支配持分に配分する。非支配持分の取り分 = 30% × $500,000 = $150,000。この額は連結損益計算書の本体に非支配持分に帰属する純利益として表示し、支配持分には残りの$350,000を配分する。

questions.stem: Heron Corp. issues 10,000 shares of $1 par common stock and 2,000 shares of $20 par preferred stock for a lump-sum payment of $190,000. At the issuance date, the common stock has a fair value of $12 per share and the preferred stock has a fair value of $40 per share. Using the proportional method, what amount of the proceeds should be allocated to the common stock?
questions.explanation: When both classes have determinable fair values, the lump-sum proceeds are allocated proportionally based on relative fair values. Total fair value = (10,000 × $12) + (2,000 × $40) = $120,000 + $80,000 = $200,000. Common stock allocation = ($120,000 / $200,000) × $190,000 = $114,000. $120,000 is the full fair value of the common stock without scaling to the actual proceeds. $76,000 is the amount allocated to the preferred stock, not the common. $95,000 results from incorrectly splitting the proceeds 50/50 between the two classes.
questions.explanationJa: 両クラスの公正価値が測定可能な場合、一括発行の対価は相対公正価値に基づき比例配分する（按分法）。公正価値合計 = (10,000株 × $12) + (2,000株 × $40) = $120,000 + $80,000 = $200,000。普通株式への配分額 = ($120,000 / $200,000) × $190,000 = $114,000。$120,000は受取対価への按分を行わない普通株式の公正価値そのもの、$76,000は優先株式への配分額、$95,000は単純な折半による誤りである。

questions.stem: Osprey Inc. issues 8,000 shares of common stock and 1,000 shares of preferred stock for a total of $260,000. The common stock is actively traded at $25 per share, but no reliable fair value is available for the newly created class of preferred stock. What amount should be allocated to the preferred stock?
questions.explanation: When only one class of securities has a determinable fair value, the incremental method is used: the security with the known fair value is assigned that value, and the remainder of the proceeds is allocated to the other security. Common stock = 8,000 × $25 = $200,000. Preferred stock = $260,000 − $200,000 = $60,000. $200,000 is the amount assigned to the common stock, not the preferred. $130,000 results from incorrectly splitting the proceeds equally. $26,000 has no basis in the allocation.
questions.explanationJa: 一方のクラスのみ公正価値が測定可能な場合は増分法（incremental method）を用いる。公正価値が判明している証券にその金額を配分し、残額をもう一方に配分する。普通株式 = 8,000株 × $25 = $200,000。優先株式 = $260,000 − $200,000 = $60,000。$200,000は普通株式への配分額であり、$130,000は折半による誤りである。

questions.stem: Kestrel Corp. issues 4,000 shares of its $5 par common stock in exchange for a parcel of land. Kestrel's stock is actively traded on a national exchange at $22 per share on the exchange date. The seller had the land independently appraised at $92,000, but the appraisal is considered less reliable than the quoted stock price. What amount should Kestrel credit to additional paid-in capital?
questions.explanation: Stock issued for noncash assets is recorded at the more reliably determinable of the fair value of the stock issued or the fair value of the asset received. Because the stock is actively traded, its quoted price is the better measure: 4,000 × $22 = $88,000 total. Common stock (par) = 4,000 × $5 = $20,000, so APIC = $88,000 − $20,000 = $68,000. $72,000 results from using the $92,000 appraisal ($92,000 − $20,000). $88,000 is the total recorded value of the land, not the APIC portion. $20,000 is the par value credited to common stock.
questions.explanationJa: 現物出資により発行した株式は、発行株式の公正価値と受入資産の公正価値のうち、より信頼性をもって測定可能な金額で記録する。本問では活発に取引される株式の市場価格が優先される: 4,000株 × $22 = $88,000。額面（普通株式勘定）= 4,000株 × $5 = $20,000。よってAPIC = $88,000 − $20,000 = $68,000。$72,000は鑑定評価額$92,000を用いた誤り、$88,000は土地の計上総額、$20,000は額面部分である。

questions.stem: Plover Corp. issues 1,500 shares of its $1 par common stock to its outside attorney in full settlement of legal services rendered in connection with a lawsuit. On the issuance date, Plover's stock trades at $14 per share. What amount of legal expense should Plover recognize?
questions.explanation: Stock issued in exchange for services is recorded at the fair value of the stock issued (or the services received, if more reliably measurable). Legal expense = 1,500 × $14 = $21,000, with $1,500 credited to common stock at par and $19,500 to APIC. $1,500 is only the par value, $19,500 is only the APIC portion, and $0 ignores that consideration in the form of stock still gives rise to an expense for services consumed.
questions.explanationJa: 役務の対価として発行した株式は、発行株式の公正価値（または役務の公正価値のうち信頼性の高い方）で記録する。法務費用 = 1,500株 × $14 = $21,000。仕訳は普通株式（額面）$1,500、APIC $19,500の貸方計上となる。$1,500は額面のみ、$19,500はAPIC部分のみであり、株式での支払いでも費消した役務は費用認識が必要なため$0は誤り。

questions.subtopic: Stock Issuance Costs - Direct vs Indirect
questions.stem: Which of the following costs incurred in connection with the issuance of common stock should be expensed as incurred rather than treated as a reduction of the offering proceeds?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Underwriting commissions paid to the investment bank

choices.label: B
choices.text: Salaries of management for time spent on the stock offering

choices.label: C
choices.text: SEC registration fees for the shares issued

choices.label: D
choices.text: Legal fees directly attributable to drafting the offering documents

questions.explanation: Only direct, incremental costs of issuing equity (underwriting commissions, registration fees, and directly attributable legal and accounting fees) are charged against the gross proceeds as a reduction of additional paid-in capital. Indirect costs, such as management salaries and allocated overhead, would have been incurred regardless of the offering and must be expensed as incurred. Underwriting commissions, SEC registration fees, and directly attributable legal fees are all direct incremental costs netted against APIC.
questions.explanationJa: 株式発行に直接起因する増分費用（引受手数料、SEC登録費用、募集書類作成の弁護士費用等）のみが発行対価から控除されAPICの減額として処理される（SAB Topic 5.A）。一方、経営者の給与や間接費の配賦のような間接費用は、発行の有無にかかわらず発生するものであり、発生時に費用処理しなければならない。引受手数料・登録費用・直接帰属する法務費用はいずれもAPIC控除の対象となる直接増分費用である。

questions.subtopic: Treasury Stock - Par Value Method Acquisition
questions.stem: Egret Corp. originally issued 10,000 shares of $10 par common stock at $18 per share. Egret later reacquires 1,000 of these shares at $22 per share and accounts for treasury stock using the par value method. There is no additional paid-in capital from prior treasury stock transactions. What amount should Egret debit to retained earnings for the reacquisition?
questions.explanation: Under the par value method, treasury stock is debited at par (1,000 × $10 = $10,000) and the APIC recorded on original issuance is removed (1,000 × ($18 − $10) = $8,000). Cash paid is $22,000. Because the cost ($22,000) exceeds the original issue proceeds removed ($18,000), the $4,000 excess is debited to retained earnings. $12,000 is the entire excess of cost over par ($22 − $10) × 1,000, ignoring the APIC removal. $8,000 is the original-issue APIC that is debited to APIC, not retained earnings. $0 would be the retained earnings effect under the cost method, not the par value method.
questions.explanationJa: 額面法では、自己株式を額面で借方計上し（1,000株 × $10 = $10,000）、当初発行時のAPICを取り崩す（1,000株 × ($18 − $10) = $8,000）。支払額$22,000が当初発行対価$18,000を超える$4,000は利益剰余金の借方となる。$12,000は取得価額と額面の差額全体でAPIC取崩しを無視した誤り、$8,000はAPIC勘定の借方計上額（利益剰余金ではない）、$0は原価法を適用した場合の利益剰余金への影響である。

questions.subtopic: Treasury Stock - Effect on Equity and Income
questions.stem: Curlew Corp. purchases treasury stock during Year 1 under the cost method and reissues all of the shares during Year 2 at a price above their acquisition cost. Which of the following correctly describes the effects of these transactions?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The purchase decreases total stockholders' equity, and no gain is reported in net income on the reissuance

choices.label: B
choices.text: The purchase has no effect on total stockholders' equity, and a gain is reported in net income on the reissuance

choices.label: C
choices.text: The purchase decreases total stockholders' equity, and a gain is reported in net income on the reissuance

choices.label: D
choices.text: The purchase increases total stockholders' equity, and the excess on reissuance is credited to retained earnings

questions.explanation: A treasury stock purchase reduces total stockholders' equity by the cost of the shares because treasury stock is a contra-equity account. An entity never recognizes gains or losses in net income on transactions in its own stock; when treasury shares are reissued above cost, the excess is credited to additional paid-in capital from treasury stock transactions, not to income and not to retained earnings. The statements that report a gain in net income, that the purchase has no equity effect, or that equity increases on purchase all contradict these principles.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式の取得は、自己株式が株主資本の控除項目であるため、取得原価分だけ株主資本合計を減少させる。自社株式に係る取引から損益を純利益に計上することは認められない（ASC 505-30）。取得原価を超えて再発行した場合の超過額は自己株式取引によるAPICに貸方計上され、純利益にも利益剰余金にも計上されない。純利益への利得計上、取得時に株主資本へ影響なし、取得で株主資本増加とする記述はいずれも誤り。

questions.subtopic: Treasury Stock - Par Value Method Reissuance
questions.stem: Avocet Corp. uses the par value method to account for treasury stock. It holds 500 treasury shares of $10 par common stock, carried at par. Avocet reissues all 500 shares for $17 per share. What amount should be credited to additional paid-in capital on the reissuance?
questions.explanation: Under the par value method, a reissuance of treasury stock is treated like a new issuance of shares: cash is debited for the proceeds (500 × $17 = $8,500), treasury stock is credited at par (500 × $10 = $5,000), and the excess of $3,500 is credited to additional paid-in capital. $5,000 is the credit to the treasury stock account at par. $8,500 is the total cash received. $0 would ignore the premium over par received on reissuance.
questions.explanationJa: 額面法では自己株式の再発行を新規発行と同様に処理する。現金 $8,500（500株 × $17）を借方、自己株式を額面 $5,000（500株 × $10）で貸方計上し、差額 $3,500 をAPICに貸方計上する。$5,000は自己株式勘定の貸方額、$8,500は受取現金総額であり、額面超過分が生じているため$0は誤り。

questions.subtopic: Stock Split Effected in the Form of a Dividend
questions.stem: Godwit Corp. has 100,000 shares of $5 par common stock outstanding with a market price of $20 per share. To comply with stock exchange requirements, Godwit declares a 100% stock dividend that is accounted for as a stock split effected in the form of a dividend, with no change in par value. What amount should Godwit transfer from retained earnings to contributed capital?
questions.explanation: A stock split effected in the form of a dividend leaves the par value per share unchanged, so legal requirements obligate the entity to capitalize an amount equal to the par value of the additional shares issued: 100,000 new shares × $5 par = $500,000, transferred from retained earnings to common stock. $2,000,000 (100,000 × $20 market) would apply to a small stock dividend, which is capitalized at fair value. $0 would apply to a true stock split in which par value per share is reduced proportionately and only a memo entry is made. $1,500,000 is the difference between the market-value and par-value capitalizations and has no basis.
questions.explanationJa: 配当形式による株式分割（stock split effected in the form of a dividend）では1株当たり額面が変わらないため、法定資本の要請から、増加株式の額面相当額を利益剰余金から資本金へ振り替える: 新株100,000株 × 額面$5 = $500,000。$2,000,000（市場価格基準）は小規模株式配当に適用される処理であり、$0は額面を比例的に引き下げる純粋な株式分割（メモ記入のみ）の場合である。

questions.stem: On November 1, Year 1, Dunlin Corp.'s board of directors declares a cash dividend payable on January 15, Year 2, to shareholders of record on December 1, Year 1. What journal entry, if any, should Dunlin record on December 1, Year 1, the date of record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit retained earnings and credit dividends payable

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit dividends payable and credit cash

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit retained earnings and credit cash

choices.label: D
choices.text: No journal entry is required

questions.explanation: A cash dividend involves three dates: the declaration date, when retained earnings is debited and dividends payable is credited because a legal liability arises; the date of record, which merely identifies the shareholders entitled to the dividend and requires no journal entry; and the payment date, when dividends payable is debited and cash is credited. The entry debiting retained earnings and crediting dividends payable belongs on the declaration date, and the entry debiting dividends payable and crediting cash belongs on the payment date.
questions.explanationJa: 現金配当には3つの日付がある。宣言日には法的債務が発生するため利益剰余金を借方、未払配当金を貸方に計上する。基準日（date of record）は配当を受け取る株主を特定するだけの日であり、仕訳は不要である。支払日には未払配当金を借方、現金を貸方に計上する。利益剰余金/未払配当金の仕訳は宣言日、未払配当金/現金の仕訳は支払日の処理である。

questions.subtopic: Property Dividend - Asset Carried Above Fair Value
questions.stem: Sanderling Corp. declares a property dividend, distributable to common shareholders, consisting of a parcel of land with a carrying amount of $200,000 and a fair value of $170,000 at the declaration date. What amount of loss, if any, should Sanderling recognize in net income as a result of the declaration?
questions.explanation: A nonmonetary asset distributed as a property dividend is remeasured to fair value at the declaration date, and any gain or loss is recognized in net income. The loss is $200,000 − $170,000 = $30,000, and retained earnings is then charged for the $170,000 fair value of the dividend. $0 incorrectly assumes the asset is transferred at carrying amount with no remeasurement. $170,000 is the charge to retained earnings, not a loss in income. $200,000 is the carrying amount of the land, which is neither the loss nor the dividend amount.
questions.explanationJa: 財産配当として分配する非貨幣性資産は宣言日に公正価値へ再測定し、評価損益を純利益に認識する（ASC 845-10-30）。損失 = $200,000 − $170,000 = $30,000。その後、配当の公正価値$170,000を利益剰余金から減額する。$0は帳簿価額のまま移転すると誤解した場合、$170,000は利益剰余金の減額（損失ではない）、$200,000は土地の帳簿価額である。

questions.stem: Turnstone Corp. has 60,000 shares of $2 par common stock outstanding. It declares and distributes a 15% stock dividend when the market price of the stock is $25 per share. What amount should Turnstone credit to additional paid-in capital for the stock dividend?
questions.explanation: A stock dividend of less than 20–25% is a small stock dividend, capitalized at fair value. New shares = 60,000 × 15% = 9,000. Retained earnings is debited for 9,000 × $25 = $225,000; common stock is credited at par for 9,000 × $2 = $18,000; and the difference of $207,000 is credited to APIC. $225,000 is the total retained earnings charge, not the APIC portion. $18,000 is the par value credited to common stock. $0 would apply only to a large stock dividend capitalized at par, where no APIC arises.
questions.explanationJa: 20〜25%未満の株式配当は小規模株式配当として公正価値で資本化する。新株 = 60,000株 × 15% = 9,000株。利益剰余金の借方 = 9,000株 × $25 = $225,000、普通株式（額面）の貸方 = 9,000株 × $2 = $18,000、差額 $207,000 がAPICへの貸方計上額となる。$225,000は利益剰余金の減額総額、$18,000は額面部分、$0は額面で資本化する大規模株式配当の場合である。

questions.stem: On December 15, Year 1, Whimbrel Corp. declares a 10% stock dividend to be distributed on January 20, Year 2. At December 31, Year 1, how should Whimbrel report the common stock dividend distributable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a current liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a component of stockholders' equity

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a long-term liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a deduction from retained earnings disclosed only in the notes

questions.explanation: A stock dividend declared but not yet distributed is reported as common stock dividend distributable within stockholders' equity, typically immediately after common stock. It is not a liability because it will be settled by issuing the entity's own shares rather than by transferring assets. Reporting it as a current or long-term liability is therefore incorrect, and retained earnings has already been formally reduced by the journal entry at declaration, so a note-only deduction is also incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: 宣言済み未交付の株式配当は「交付予定株式配当（common stock dividend distributable）」として株主資本の部（通常は普通株式の直後）に表示する。資産の引渡しではなく自社株式の交付により決済されるため負債には該当しない。流動負債・固定負債としての表示は誤りであり、利益剰余金は宣言時の仕訳で既に減額されているため注記のみの開示も誤りである。

questions.stem: Phalarope Corp. has retained earnings of $300,000 and additional paid-in capital of $900,000. The board declares a cash dividend of $500,000, and state law permits dividends to be charged against paid-in capital once retained earnings is exhausted. What amount of the dividend should be debited to additional paid-in capital?
questions.explanation: A dividend in excess of retained earnings is a liquidating dividend to the extent of the excess, representing a return of capital rather than a distribution of earnings. Retained earnings absorbs the first $300,000, and the remaining $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000 is debited to additional paid-in capital. $500,000 would charge the entire dividend to APIC, ignoring available retained earnings. $300,000 is the portion charged to retained earnings. $0 ignores that the dividend exceeds retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 利益剰余金を超える配当は、超過部分が清算配当（liquidating dividend）となり、利益の分配ではなく資本の払戻しを意味する。まず利益剰余金$300,000を全額充当し、残額 $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000 をAPICの借方に計上する。$500,000は全額をAPICに賦課する誤り、$300,000は利益剰余金への賦課額、$0は配当が利益剰余金を超過している事実を無視した誤りである。

questions.stem: Willet Corp. operates in a state whose corporation law prohibits dividend distributions that would reduce total stockholders' equity below legal capital, defined as the aggregate par value of issued shares. Willet has total stockholders' equity of $2,400,000, which includes common stock (par) of $1,500,000. What is the maximum cash dividend Willet may legally declare?
questions.explanation: Under a legal capital statute, distributions may not reduce total stockholders' equity below the aggregate par value of issued shares. The maximum dividend is therefore $2,400,000 − $1,500,000 = $900,000. $2,400,000 would distribute all equity including legal capital. $1,500,000 is the legal capital itself, which must be preserved rather than distributed. $0 is incorrect because equity exceeds legal capital, leaving a distributable surplus.
questions.explanationJa: 法定資本制度の下では、配当により株主資本合計を発行済株式の額面総額（法定資本）未満に減少させることはできない。よって配当可能最大額 = $2,400,000 − $1,500,000 = $900,000。$2,400,000は法定資本まで含めた全額分配であり違法、$1,500,000は維持すべき法定資本そのもの、株主資本が法定資本を上回っているため$0も誤りである。

questions.stem: Tattler Corp. has 100,000 shares of $12 par common stock issued and outstanding, with a common stock account balance of $1,200,000. Tattler effects a 3-for-1 stock split. After the split, what is the par value per share?
questions.explanation: In a true stock split, the number of shares increases and the par value per share decreases proportionately, leaving the total common stock account unchanged. Shares increase to 300,000, and the new par is $1,200,000 / 300,000 = $4 per share. $12 is the pre-split par, which does not survive a true split. $36 results from multiplying rather than dividing par by the split ratio. $6 would result from an incorrect 2-for-1 assumption.
questions.explanationJa: 純粋な株式分割では株式数が増加し、1株当たり額面が比例的に減少するため、資本金（普通株式勘定）合計は変わらない。株式数は300,000株となり、新額面 = $1,200,000 ÷ 300,000株 = $4。$12は分割前の額面、$36は分割比率を乗じてしまった誤り、$6は2対1分割と誤認した場合の値である。

questions.stem: Knot Corp. has 10,000 shares of 6%, $100 par preferred stock (total par $1,000,000) that participates in dividends up to an additional 2% above its stated rate, and common stock with a total par value of $2,000,000. No dividends are in arrears. Knot declares total dividends of $300,000. What total amount should be allocated to the preferred shareholders?
questions.explanation: Preferred first receives its stated rate: 6% × $1,000,000 = $60,000. Common then receives a matching 6% × $2,000,000 = $120,000. The remaining $300,000 − $180,000 = $120,000 would be shared pro rata on par values ($120,000 / $3,000,000 = 4%), but the preferred participation is capped at an additional 2%, so preferred receives only 2% × $1,000,000 = $20,000 more, for a total of $80,000. $60,000 treats the preferred as nonparticipating. $100,000 results from full participation (an additional 4% with no cap). $120,000 is the matching dividend allocated to common, not the preferred total.
questions.explanationJa: 優先株式はまず表示利率分 6% × $1,000,000 = $60,000 を受け取る。普通株式に同率 6% × $2,000,000 = $120,000 を配分後、残余 $300,000 − $180,000 = $120,000 を額面比で按分すると4%相当だが、参加上限は追加2%のため優先株式の追加分は 2% × $1,000,000 = $20,000 にとどまる。優先株式合計 = $60,000 + $20,000 = $80,000。$60,000は非参加型とした場合、$100,000は完全参加型（上限なし追加4%）とした場合、$120,000は普通株式への同率配分額である。

questions.stem: Ruff Corp. has 5,000 shares of 8%, $100 par noncumulative, nonparticipating preferred stock and 60,000 shares of common stock outstanding. No dividends were declared in Year 1 or Year 2. In Year 3, Ruff declares total dividends of $190,000. What amount should be distributed to the common shareholders?
questions.explanation: Noncumulative preferred stock has no claim to dividends omitted in prior years; passed dividends are lost permanently. In Year 3, the preferred is entitled only to the current year's preference of 8% × $100 × 5,000 = $40,000, leaving $190,000 − $40,000 = $150,000 for common. $70,000 results from incorrectly treating the preferred as cumulative and paying three years of dividends ($120,000) first. $40,000 is the preferred allocation, not the common. $190,000 ignores the preferred's current-year preference entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 非累積型優先株式は、過年度に宣言されなかった配当に対する請求権を持たず、未宣言分は永久に消滅する。Year 3では当年度分 8% × $100 × 5,000株 = $40,000 のみが優先され、普通株式への配分 = $190,000 − $40,000 = $150,000。$70,000は累積型と誤認して3年分$120,000を先取りさせた場合、$40,000は優先株式への配分額、$190,000は当年度の優先権すら無視した誤りである。

questions.stem: Stint Corp., an SEC registrant, issues preferred stock that is redeemable for cash at the option of the holder. The shares are not mandatorily redeemable on a fixed date. How should Stint classify the preferred stock in its balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Outside of permanent equity, in temporary (mezzanine) equity

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a long-term liability under ASC 480

choices.label: C
choices.text: Within permanent stockholders' equity, combined with other preferred stock

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a current liability, because the holder may redeem at any time

questions.explanation: Preferred stock redeemable at the holder's option (or upon events outside the issuer's control) is not mandatorily redeemable, so it does not meet the ASC 480 criteria for liability classification. However, under ASC 480-10-S99 (SEC ASR 268), SEC registrants must present such shares outside of permanent equity, in temporary or mezzanine equity, because redemption is not solely within the issuer's control. Liability classification (long-term or current) applies only to unconditionally mandatorily redeemable shares, and inclusion in permanent equity is prohibited for SEC registrants when redemption can be triggered by the holder.
questions.explanationJa: 保有者の選択（または発行者が支配できない事象）により償還可能な優先株式は、無条件の強制償還株式ではないためASC 480の負債分類要件を満たさない。しかしSEC登録企業は、償還が発行者の支配下にないため、ASC 480-10-S99（SEC ASR 268）により恒久資本の外、すなわち仮勘定資本（mezzanine equity）として表示しなければならない。負債分類（固定・流動とも）は無条件の強制償還株式に限られ、保有者が償還を発動できる場合に恒久株主資本へ含めることはSEC登録企業には認められない。

questions.subtopic: Stock Subscription - Subscribed Stock and Receivable Balances
questions.stem: Brant Corp. receives subscriptions for 6,000 shares of its $10 par common stock at a subscription price of $20 per share, collecting a 40% down payment in cash. The shares will not be issued until the full subscription price is collected. At the balance sheet date, what amount should Brant report as common stock subscribed?
questions.explanation: Common stock subscribed is credited at the par value of the subscribed shares: 6,000 × $10 = $60,000. The excess of the subscription price over par (6,000 × ($20 − $10) = $60,000) is credited to APIC at the subscription date, and subscriptions receivable is debited for the full $120,000, reduced by the $48,000 collected to a $72,000 remaining balance. $120,000 is the total subscription price, not the subscribed stock account. $48,000 is the cash collected. $72,000 is the remaining subscriptions receivable balance.
questions.explanationJa: 申込済株式（common stock subscribed）勘定は申込株式の額面で貸方計上する: 6,000株 × $10 = $60,000。額面超過分（6,000株 × ($20 − $10) = $60,000）は申込時にAPICへ貸方計上し、株式申込未収金は総額$120,000で借方計上後、入金$48,000を控除した残高$72,000となる。$120,000は申込価額総額、$48,000は入金額、$72,000は未収金残高である。

questions.stem: Murre Corp. has retained earnings of $1,000,000. The board of directors votes to appropriate $300,000 of retained earnings for a planned plant expansion. Immediately after the appropriation, what amount should Murre report as total retained earnings?
questions.explanation: An appropriation merely reclassifies retained earnings into appropriated and unappropriated portions; it does not reduce total retained earnings, set aside cash, or create an expense or liability. Total retained earnings remains $1,000,000, consisting of $300,000 appropriated and $700,000 unappropriated. $700,000 is only the unappropriated portion. $300,000 is only the appropriated portion. $1,300,000 incorrectly adds the appropriation to the existing balance.
questions.explanationJa: 利益剰余金の充当（appropriation）は利益剰余金を充当済と未充当に区分表示するだけであり、利益剰余金総額の減少、現金の留保、費用や負債の計上は一切生じない。総額は$1,000,000のまま（充当済$300,000＋未充当$700,000）。$700,000は未充当部分のみ、$300,000は充当済部分のみ、$1,300,000は充当額を加算してしまった誤りである。

questions.stem: Fulmar Corp. undertakes a quasi-reorganization. Before the reorganization, Fulmar has an accumulated deficit of $1,800,000 and additional paid-in capital of $200,000. As part of the procedures, assets are written down to fair value, increasing the deficit by $400,000. The par value of its 500,000 outstanding shares is then reduced from $10 to $4 per share, with the reduction credited to additional paid-in capital, which absorbs the entire deficit. What is the balance of additional paid-in capital immediately after the quasi-reorganization?
questions.explanation: The par reduction creates APIC of 500,000 × ($10 − $4) = $3,000,000, bringing APIC to $200,000 + $3,000,000 = $3,200,000. The deficit after the asset write-down is $1,800,000 + $400,000 = $2,200,000, which is charged against APIC, leaving $3,200,000 − $2,200,000 = $1,000,000. Retained earnings is reset to zero and dated. $1,400,000 omits the $400,000 asset write-down from the deficit. $800,000 omits the pre-existing $200,000 APIC. $3,200,000 is the APIC before absorbing the deficit.

questions.stem: Gannet Corp. reports the following for Year 1: net income $400,000; unrealized holding gain on available-for-sale debt securities $30,000; foreign currency translation loss $20,000; unrealized holding gain on trading securities $10,000 (included in net income); and prior service cost arising during the year from a pension plan amendment $15,000. What amount should Gannet report as comprehensive income for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Comprehensive income = net income + other comprehensive income. OCI includes the unrealized gain on AFS debt securities (+$30,000), the translation loss (−$20,000), and the prior service cost arising during the year (−$15,000), for net OCI of −$5,000. Comprehensive income = $400,000 − $5,000 = $395,000. The trading securities gain is already in net income and is never an OCI item; adding it again gives the incorrect $405,000. $410,000 omits the pension prior service cost. $445,000 incorrectly adds all items as gains, ignoring the signs of the translation loss and prior service cost.
questions.explanationJa: 包括利益 = 純利益 + その他の包括利益（OCI）。OCI = AFS負債証券の未実現利得 +$30,000、外貨換算損失 −$20,000、当期発生の過去勤務費用 −$15,000 = 純額 −$5,000。包括利益 = $400,000 − $5,000 = $395,000。売買目的有価証券の利得は既に純利益に含まれOCI項目ではないため、二重計上すると$405,000となる。$410,000は過去勤務費用の見落とし、$445,000は損失項目の符号を誤って全て加算した場合である。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Changes in Stockholders' Equity
questions.stem: Under U.S. GAAP, when an entity presents both financial position and results of operations, what disclosure is required with respect to changes in stockholders' equity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only changes in retained earnings must be disclosed

choices.label: B
choices.text: Changes in equity accounts need not be disclosed if a balance sheet is presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: Changes in the separate accounts comprising stockholders' equity must be disclosed, either in a separate statement or in the notes

choices.label: D
choices.text: A separate statement of stockholders' equity is required as a basic financial statement in all cases

questions.explanation: ASC 505-10-50 requires disclosure of changes in the separate accounts comprising stockholders' equity (such as common stock, APIC, retained earnings, treasury stock, and AOCI) and in the number of shares, for each period for which an income statement is presented. The disclosure may be made in a separate statement of stockholders' equity, in the basic statements, or in the notes. Disclosing only retained earnings changes is insufficient, omitting the disclosure entirely is not permitted, and a separate statement is one acceptable format rather than the only required one.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 505-10-50は、損益計算書が表示される各期間について、株主資本を構成する各勘定（普通株式、APIC、利益剰余金、自己株式、AOCI等）の増減および株式数の変動の開示を要求する。開示方法は株主資本等変動計算書、基本財務諸表内、または注記のいずれでもよい。利益剰余金の変動のみの開示では不十分であり、開示の省略は認められず、独立の計算書は許容される形式の一つであって唯一の必須形式ではない。

questions.subtopic: Convertible Preferred Conversion - Book Value Method
questions.stem: Jaeger Corp. has 2,000 shares of $50 par convertible preferred stock outstanding that were originally issued for $55 per share. Each preferred share is convertible into 5 shares of $12 par common stock. All preferred shares are converted when the common stock's market price is $20 per share. Under the book value method, what amount should Jaeger debit to retained earnings on the conversion?
questions.explanation: Under the book value method, the carrying amount of the preferred (2,000 × $55 = $110,000) is transferred to common equity with no gain or loss. Common shares issued = 2,000 × 5 = 10,000 shares, with total par of 10,000 × $12 = $120,000. Because the par of the common issued ($120,000) exceeds the preferred's carrying amount ($110,000), the $10,000 shortfall is debited to retained earnings (no APIC credit arises). $0 would be correct only if the common par were less than or equal to the preferred carrying amount. $120,000 is the par of the common issued. $90,000 incorrectly uses the $20 market price (10,000 × $20 = $200,000 less $110,000), which belongs to the market value method, and is also miscomputed.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿価額法では、優先株式の帳簿価額（2,000株 × $55 = $110,000）を損益を認識せずに普通株式資本へ振り替える。発行する普通株式 = 2,000株 × 5 = 10,000株、額面合計 = 10,000株 × $12 = $120,000。普通株式の額面$120,000が優先株式の帳簿価額$110,000を上回るため、不足額$10,000を利益剰余金の借方に計上する（APICへの貸方は生じない）。$0は普通株式の額面が帳簿価額以下の場合にのみ正しい。$120,000は発行普通株式の額面総額である。

questions.stem: Skua Corp. issues preferred stock that is callable at a fixed price solely at the option of Skua, the issuer. The shares have no mandatory redemption date and are not redeemable at the holder's option. How should Skua classify the callable preferred stock?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability, because the call price represents a future cash obligation

choices.label: B
choices.text: In temporary (mezzanine) equity, because the shares may be redeemed

choices.label: C
choices.text: As equity until the call is announced, then as a current liability retroactively for all periods presented

choices.label: D
choices.text: Within permanent stockholders' equity, with the call price disclosed

questions.explanation: Preferred stock callable solely at the issuer's option imposes no obligation on the issuer—redemption is entirely within its control—so the shares are neither mandatorily redeemable liabilities under ASC 480 nor mezzanine equity under the SEC guidance, which applies when redemption is outside the issuer's control.
