The shares remain in permanent equity, with disclosure of the call (redemption) price. Liability treatment applies to mandatorily redeemable shares, mezzanine presentation applies when the holder or an external event can force redemption, and retroactive reclassification of prior periods upon a call announcement is not a GAAP concept.
questions.explanationJa: 発行者のみが行使できるコール条項付き優先株式は、償還が完全に発行者の支配下にあり発行者に義務を課さないため、ASC 480の強制償還負債にも、償還が発行者の支配外にある場合に適用されるSECのメザニン表示ガイダンスにも該当しない。恒久株主資本に含めて表示し、買戻（償還）価格を開示する。負債分類は強制償還株式、メザニン表示は保有者や外部事象が償還を強制できる場合の処理であり、コール公表時に過年度へ遡及して負債に再分類する処理はGAAP上存在しない。

questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Petrel Corp. declares a scrip dividend by issuing promissory notes to shareholders totaling $300,000, bearing interest at 10% and payable on October 1, Year 2. What amount should be charged directly to retained earnings as a result of the scrip dividend declaration?
questions.explanation: A scrip dividend is recorded at the declaration date by debiting retained earnings and crediting notes (scrip) payable for the principal amount of $300,000. Interest on the scrip is a financing cost recognized as interest expense over the period the notes are outstanding; it reduces net income, not retained earnings directly. $307,500 incorrectly adds three months of accrued interest ($7,500) to the retained earnings charge. $330,000 incorrectly adds a full year of interest ($30,000). $0 ignores that the declaration creates an immediate charge to retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: スクリップ配当は宣言日に元本額$300,000で利益剰余金を借方、スクリップ（約束手形）未払金を貸方に計上する。手形利息は手形の未決済期間にわたり支払利息として費用認識され、純利益を減少させるが、利益剰余金への直接賦課項目ではない。$307,500は3か月分の経過利息$7,500を、$330,000は1年分の利息$30,000を誤って利益剰余金賦課額に含めた場合であり、宣言時に利益剰余金への賦課が生じるため$0も誤りである。

questions.stem: Gadwall Corp. issues 8,000 shares of true no-par common stock (no stated value) for $35 per share. What amount should Gadwall credit to the common stock account?
questions.explanation: When true no-par stock without a stated value is issued, the entire proceeds are credited to the common stock account: 8,000 × $35 = $280,000. No additional paid-in capital arises. $40,000 assumes a $5 stated value, which would apply only to no-par stock with a stated value. $240,000 would be the APIC under a $5 stated-value assumption, but no APIC is recorded for true no-par stock. $0 is incorrect because the proceeds must be credited to common stock.
questions.explanationJa: 表示価額（stated value）のない純粋な無額面株式を発行した場合、受取対価の全額を普通株式勘定に貸方計上する: 8,000株 × $35 = $280,000。APICは生じない。$40,000は表示価額$5を仮定した場合の処理（表示価額付き無額面株式にのみ適用）、$240,000は同じ仮定でのAPIC相当額であり、純粋な無額面株式ではAPICは計上されない。

questions.stem: Smew Corp. begins Year 1 with a credit balance of $50,000 in accumulated other comprehensive income. During Year 1: an unrealized holding gain of $25,000 arises on available-for-sale debt securities; a $10,000 gain previously recognized in OCI is reclassified to net income upon sale of securities; and a foreign currency translation loss of $8,000 arises. Ignoring income taxes, what is the ending balance of AOCI?
questions.explanation: The reclassification adjustment removes from AOCI the $10,000 gain now realized in net income, preventing double counting. Ending AOCI = $50,000 + $25,000 − $10,000 − $8,000 = $57,000. $67,000 omits the reclassification adjustment. $77,000 incorrectly adds the reclassified gain back to AOCI instead of removing it. $32,000 incorrectly subtracts the $25,000 unrealized gain rather than adding it.
questions.explanationJa: 組替調整（reclassification adjustment）は、純利益に実現計上された$10,000の利得をAOCIから除去し、二重計上を防ぐ。期末AOCI = $50,000 + $25,000 − $10,000 − $8,000 = $57,000。$67,000は組替調整の見落とし、$77,000は組替分を除去せず逆に加算した誤り、$32,000は未実現利得$25,000を加算ではなく減算した誤りである。

questions.stem: Wigeon Corp. has 1,000,000 shares of common stock authorized and 400,000 shares issued, of which 25,000 shares are held in treasury. Wigeon declares a cash dividend of $0.60 per share. What total amount of dividends should Wigeon record at the declaration date?
questions.explanation: Cash dividends are paid only on shares outstanding; treasury shares are issued but not outstanding and receive no dividends. Outstanding shares = 400,000 − 25,000 = 375,000, so the dividend is 375,000 × $0.60 = $225,000. $240,000 incorrectly pays the dividend on all issued shares including treasury shares. $600,000 incorrectly uses the authorized shares. $15,000 is the dividend that would relate to the treasury shares alone (25,000 × $0.60), which is precisely the amount excluded.
questions.explanationJa: 現金配当は発行済かつ社外流通株式（outstanding shares）に対してのみ支払われる。自己株式は発行済みだが流通しておらず配当を受け取らない。流通株式数 = 400,000 − 25,000 = 375,000株、配当総額 = 375,000株 × $0.60 = $225,000。$240,000は自己株式を含む発行済株式全体に配当した誤り、$600,000は授権株式数を用いた誤り、$15,000は除外されるべき自己株式分（25,000株 × $0.60）である。

questions.stem: Falcon Corp.'s board of directors declares a cash dividend of $1.50 per share on November 1, Year 1. The date of record is November 20, the ex-dividend date set by the stock exchange is November 18, and the dividend is payable on December 10. An investor purchases 100 shares of Falcon in the open market on November 19, Year 1, and continues to hold them through December 10. Which of the following statements is correct regarding this dividend?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The investor receives the dividend because the shares are held on the payment date.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The investor receives the dividend because the purchase occurred before the date of record.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The seller receives the dividend because the purchase occurred on or after the ex-dividend date.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither party receives the dividend because the shares changed hands between the declaration date and the payment date.

questions.explanation: The ex-dividend date is the cutoff for entitlement to a declared dividend. Shares purchased on or after the ex-dividend date (November 18) trade without the right to the dividend, so the seller — not the buyer — receives it, even though the purchase occurred before the date of record. Holding shares on the payment date is irrelevant; entitlement is fixed by ownership before the ex-dividend date. The statement that the buyer is entitled because the purchase preceded the record date ignores the settlement-period mechanics that the ex-dividend date is designed to address, and the statement that neither party receives the dividend is wrong because the dividend, once declared, is a legal liability that will be paid to the holder of record (here, effectively the seller).
questions.explanationJa: 配当落ち日（ex-dividend date）は、宣言済み配当を受け取る権利の実質的な締切日である。配当落ち日（11月18日）以降に購入した株式は配当を受ける権利なしで取引されるため、基準日（11月20日）より前の購入であっても、配当を受け取るのは買い手ではなく売り手である。支払日に株式を保有しているかどうかは無関係で、権利は配当落ち日前の所有によって確定する。「基準日前の購入だから買い手が受け取る」という考えは、決済期間を考慮して設定される配当落ち日の仕組みを無視している。また、宣言された配当は法的債務であり必ず権利者（実質的に売り手）に支払われるため、「誰も受け取らない」ということはない。

questions.subtopic: Lump-Sum Issuance - Stock with Detachable Warrants
questions.stem: Pelican Corp. sells 5,000 units for $50 per unit. Each unit consists of one share of $5 par common stock and one detachable warrant to purchase an additional common share. On the issuance date, the common stock trades at $48 per share and the warrants trade at $12 each. Using the relative fair value (proportional) method, what amount should Pelican credit to additional paid-in capital—common stock?
questions.explanation: Total proceeds = 5,000 units × $50 = $250,000. Total fair value per unit = $48 + $12 = $60. The common stock's share = $48 / $60 = 80%, so $250,000 × 80% = $200,000 is allocated to the common stock. Of that amount, par value of 5,000 × $5 = $25,000 is credited to common stock, leaving $200,000 − $25,000 = $175,000 credited to APIC—common stock. The $200,000 figure is the total allocated to common before removing par value. The $225,000 figure incorrectly deducts par from total proceeds without first allocating to the warrants. The $50,000 figure is the amount allocated to the warrants ($250,000 × 20%), not to common APIC.
questions.explanationJa: 総受取額＝5,000ユニット×$50＝$250,000。1ユニット当たりの公正価値合計＝$48＋$12＝$60。普通株式への配分割合＝$48÷$60＝80%なので、普通株式に$250,000×80%＝$200,000が配分される。このうち額面5,000株×$5＝$25,000が資本金（普通株式）に計上され、残りの$200,000−$25,000＝$175,000が普通株式のAPICに貸方計上される。$200,000は額面控除前の普通株式への配分総額である。$225,000はワラントへの配分を行わずに総受取額から額面のみを控除した誤りである。$50,000はワラントに配分される金額（$250,000×20%）であり、普通株式のAPICではない。

questions.subtopic: Stock Issued for Intangible Asset - Measurement
questions.stem: Cormorant Corp., whose common stock is closely held and not actively traded, issues 3,000 shares of its $10 par common stock to acquire a patent from an unrelated party. The fair value of Cormorant's stock cannot be reliably determined, but the patent's fair value, established by an independent appraisal, is $90,000. What amount should Cormorant credit to additional paid-in capital for this transaction?
questions.explanation: When stock is issued for a noncash asset, the transaction is measured at the more reliably determinable of the fair value of the stock issued or the fair value of the asset received. Because the stock is not actively traded, the patent's appraised fair value of $90,000 is used. The entry debits Patent $90,000, credits Common Stock for par of 3,000 × $10 = $30,000, and credits APIC for the remainder: $90,000 − $30,000 = $60,000. The $90,000 figure fails to separate the par value portion. The $30,000 figure is the par value credited to the common stock account, not APIC. Recording zero APIC would be appropriate only if the asset's value equaled total par, which is not the case here.
questions.explanationJa: 株式を非貨幣性資産と交換に発行する場合、発行株式の公正価値と受領資産の公正価値のうち、より信頼性をもって測定できる方で取引を測定する。本問では株式が活発に取引されていないため、独立鑑定による特許権の公正価値$90,000を用いる。仕訳は、特許権$90,000を借方計上し、額面3,000株×$10＝$30,000を資本金（普通株式）へ、残額$90,000−$30,000＝$60,000をAPICへ貸方計上する。$90,000は額面部分を区分していない誤りである。$30,000は資本金勘定に計上される額面額であり、APICではない。APICがゼロになるのは資産価値が額面総額と等しい場合のみで、本問には該当しない。

questions.stem: Bittern Corp. acquires 4,000 shares of its own common stock at $25 per share and accounts for treasury stock under the cost method. Bittern had no treasury stock transactions before this acquisition. It first reissues 1,500 of the treasury shares at $30 per share, and later reissues another 2,000 treasury shares at $18 per share. What amount should Bittern debit to retained earnings on the second reissuance?
questions.explanation: First reissuance: 1,500 shares sold at $30 against a $25 cost creates an excess of 1,500 × $5 = $7,500, credited to APIC—treasury stock. Second reissuance: 2,000 shares sold at $18 against a $25 cost creates a deficiency of 2,000 × $7 = $14,000. The deficiency is charged first to the existing APIC—treasury stock balance of $7,500, and the remaining $14,000 − $7,500 = $6,500 is debited to retained earnings. A zero charge to retained earnings would be possible only if APIC—treasury stock fully absorbed the deficiency. The $14,000 figure ignores the available APIC—treasury stock balance. The $7,500 figure is the APIC—treasury stock balance absorbed, not the retained earnings charge.
questions.explanationJa: 1回目の再発行：取得原価$25に対し1,500株を$30で売却し、超過額1,500株×$5＝$7,500を自己株式処分差益（APIC—自己株式）に貸方計上する。2回目の再発行：取得原価$25に対し2,000株を$18で売却し、不足額2,000株×$7＝$14,000が生じる。この不足額はまず既存のAPIC—自己株式残高$7,500に充当し、残り$14,000−$7,500＝$6,500を利益剰余金の借方に計上する。利益剰余金への賦課がゼロになるのはAPIC—自己株式が不足額を全額吸収できる場合のみである。$14,000はAPIC—自己株式残高の充当を無視した誤り、$7,500は充当されたAPIC—自己株式残高であって利益剰余金への賦課額ではない。

questions.subtopic: Treasury Stock - Net Effect on Total Equity
questions.stem: During Year 1, Grebe Corp. purchases 2,000 shares of its own $1 par common stock at $35 per share, accounting for the shares under the cost method. Later in the same year, Grebe reissues 1,200 of those shares at $30 per share. As a result of these two transactions, Grebe's total stockholders' equity will:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease by $70,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease by $34,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease by $6,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease by $28,000

questions.explanation: Treasury stock transactions affect total stockholders' equity by the cash paid and received. The purchase reduces equity by 2,000 × $35 = $70,000. The reissuance increases equity by the proceeds received, 1,200 × $30 = $36,000 (regardless of how the $6,000 deficiency is allocated between APIC and retained earnings within equity). Net effect: $70,000 − $36,000 = $34,000 decrease. A $70,000 decrease ignores the reissuance proceeds. A $6,000 decrease is only the reissuance deficiency (1,200 × $5) and ignores the cost of shares still held. A $28,000 decrease equals the carrying cost of the 800 shares still held (800 × $35), which omits the $6,000 deficiency absorbed within equity on the reissuance.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式取引が株主資本合計に与える影響は、支払・受取現金額で決まる。取得により株主資本は2,000株×$35＝$70,000減少する。再発行により受取額1,200株×$30＝$36,000だけ株主資本は増加する（不足額$6,000がAPICと利益剰余金のどちらに配分されるかは資本内部の振替であり合計には影響しない）。正味の影響：$70,000−$36,000＝$34,000の減少。$70,000の減少は再発行による受取額を無視している。$6,000の減少は再発行時の不足額（1,200株×$5）のみで、保有継続分の取得原価を無視している。$28,000の減少は保有継続800株の取得原価（800株×$35）に等しく、再発行時に資本内で吸収された$6,000の不足額を見落としている。

questions.subtopic: Stock Split vs. 100% Stock Dividend - Common Stock Account
questions.stem: Loon Corp. is evaluating two alternatives that would each double the number of common shares outstanding: a 2-for-1 stock split, or a 100% stock dividend accounted for as a large stock dividend capitalized at par value. Which statement correctly describes the effect of each alternative on the balance of the common stock (par) account?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The 100% stock dividend increases the common stock account, while the 2-for-1 split leaves the balance unchanged.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both alternatives increase the common stock account by the same amount.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The 2-for-1 split increases the common stock account, while the 100% stock dividend leaves the balance unchanged.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither alternative changes the balance of the common stock account.

questions.explanation: A large stock dividend transfers an amount equal to the par value of the new shares from retained earnings to the common stock account, so the common stock account balance increases. A true 2-for-1 stock split doubles the number of shares and halves the par value per share, requiring only a memorandum entry: the total dollar balance of the common stock account is unchanged. Because only the dividend changes account balances, the statements claiming both alternatives increase the account, that the split is the one that increases it, or that neither changes it are all inconsistent with this treatment.
questions.explanationJa: 大規模株式配当では、新規発行株式の額面相当額を利益剰余金から資本金（普通株式）勘定へ振り替えるため、資本金勘定の残高は増加する。一方、真正な1:2の株式分割は株式数を2倍にし1株当たり額面を半分にするだけで、備忘記録（memo entry）のみが必要であり、資本金勘定の金額残高は変わらない。勘定残高を変動させるのは株式配当のみであるため、「両方とも増加する」「分割の方が増加する」「どちらも変化しない」という記述はいずれもこの処理と整合しない。

questions.subtopic: Cumulative Fully Participating Preferred - Allocation with Arrears
questions.stem: Merganser Corp. has 6,000 shares of 4%, $100 par cumulative, fully participating preferred stock (total par $600,000) and 30,000 shares of $10 par common stock (total par $300,000) outstanding. Preferred dividends are one year in arrears. Merganser declares total dividends of $150,000 in the current year. What total amount of the dividends should be allocated to the preferred shareholders?
questions.explanation: Step 1 — Arrears to preferred: $600,000 × 4% = $24,000. Step 2 — Current-year preference to preferred: $24,000. Step 3 — Matching current rate to common: $300,000 × 4% = $12,000. Step 4 — Remainder participates pro rata by par: $150,000 − $24,000 − $24,000 − $12,000 = $90,000; preferred share = $90,000 × ($600,000 / $900,000) = $60,000. Preferred total = $24,000 + $24,000 + $60,000 = $108,000 (common receives $12,000 + $30,000 = $42,000; total checks to $150,000). The $100,000 figure omits the arrears step ($24,000 + $12,000 to common, then $114,000 × 2/3 = $76,000 to preferred). The $116,000 figure omits the matching allocation to common before participation. The $42,000 figure is the common shareholders' allocation, not the preferred allocation.

questions.subtopic: Convertible Preferred Conversion - Effect on Total Equity
questions.stem: Scaup Corp. has convertible preferred stock outstanding. During Year 1, the holders convert all of the preferred stock into common stock, and Scaup records the conversion using the book value method. On the conversion date, the fair value of the common shares issued exceeds the carrying amount of the preferred stock converted. What is the effect of the conversion on Scaup's total stockholders' equity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity increases by the excess of the common stock's fair value over the preferred stock's carrying amount.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity is unchanged.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity decreases by the excess of the common stock's fair value over the preferred stock's carrying amount.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity increases by the par value of the common shares issued.

questions.explanation: Under the book value method, the carrying amount of the preferred stock (par plus related APIC) is simply reclassified to common stock and APIC—common; no gain or loss is recognized and fair value is ignored. Because the conversion merely moves amounts between equity accounts, total stockholders' equity is unchanged. An increase equal to the fair value excess would require recognizing the shares at fair value, which the book value method does not do. A decrease is likewise impossible because nothing leaves equity. An increase equal to the par of the new common shares confuses the internal reclassification to the common stock account with a change in total equity.
questions.explanationJa: 簿価法（book value method）では、優先株式の帳簿価額（額面＋関連APIC）を普通株式の資本金と普通株式APICへ振り替えるだけであり、損益は認識せず公正価値も用いない。転換は資本勘定間の振替にすぎないため、株主資本合計は変化しない。公正価値超過額分の増加を認識するには株式を公正価値で計上する必要があるが、簿価法ではそれを行わない。資本から流出するものがないため減少も生じない。新普通株式の額面相当の増加という考えは、資本金勘定への内部振替と株主資本合計の変動を混同している。

questions.stem: Goldeneye Corp. issues preferred stock that it must redeem for cash at a fixed price on a specified date five years after issuance. Under ASC 480, Goldeneye classifies the shares as a liability. How should Goldeneye report the periodic dividend payments made on these shares?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a direct reduction of retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a charge to other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: As interest expense in the income statement

questions.explanation: Mandatorily redeemable preferred stock that embodies an unconditional obligation to redeem for cash at a fixed date is classified as a liability under ASC 480. Consistent with liability classification, the periodic payments to holders are not dividends in substance — they are reported as interest expense in the income statement. A reduction of retained earnings is the treatment for dividends on equity-classified shares. A reduction of APIC applies only when retained earnings are insufficient for an equity-classified distribution. Other comprehensive income is never used for distributions to instrument holders.
questions.explanationJa: 確定日に現金で償還する無条件の義務を伴う強制償還優先株式は、ASC 480のもとで負債に分類される。負債分類と整合的に、保有者への定期的な支払は実質的に配当ではなく、損益計算書上の支払利息（interest expense）として報告する。利益剰余金の直接減額は資本に分類される株式の配当に対する処理である。APICの減額は、資本分類の分配において利益剰余金が不足する場合に限られる。その他の包括利益が証券保有者への分配に用いられることはない。

questions.stem: Two years ago, Smelt Corp.'s board of directors appropriated $500,000 of retained earnings for a planned plant expansion. The expansion has now been completed and the appropriation is no longer needed. How should Smelt account for the $500,000 appropriation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restore the $500,000 to unappropriated retained earnings.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Charge the construction costs of the plant against the appropriation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Transfer the $500,000 to additional paid-in capital.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transfer the $500,000 to a deferred credit and amortize it to income over the plant's useful life.

questions.explanation: An appropriation of retained earnings is merely a reclassification within retained earnings that signals an intention to restrict dividends; it sets aside no assets and absorbs no costs. When the purpose for the appropriation no longer exists, the only proper treatment is to return the appropriated amount to unappropriated retained earnings. Charging construction costs against the appropriation is prohibited — costs and losses are never charged directly to an appropriation. Transferring the amount to additional paid-in capital would improperly convert earned capital into contributed capital. Transferring it to a deferred credit and amortizing it to income would improperly recognize income from what is purely an equity reclassification.
questions.explanationJa: 利益剰余金の処分（appropriation）は、配当制限の意図を示す利益剰余金内部での組替にすぎず、資産を留保するものでも費用を吸収するものでもない。処分の目的が消滅した場合の唯一の適切な処理は、処分済み金額を未処分利益剰余金に戻すことである。建設費用を処分額に賦課することは禁止されている—費用や損失を処分額に直接賦課することは認められない。APICへの振替は、稼得資本を拠出資本に転換する不適切な処理である。繰延貸方項目へ振り替えて耐用年数にわたり収益に償却する処理は、純粋な資本内組替から利益を認識する誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Property Dividend - Net Effect on Retained Earnings
questions.stem: Teal Corp. declares a property dividend, distributable to its common shareholders, consisting of a parcel of land with a carrying amount of $80,000. The land's fair value on the declaration date is $110,000. Considering both the dividend and any gain or loss recognized, what is the net effect of the property dividend on Teal's retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $110,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $80,000

questions.explanation: A property dividend is recorded at the fair value of the property on the declaration date: retained earnings is charged $110,000. The land is remeasured to fair value, and a gain of $110,000 − $80,000 = $30,000 is recognized in income, which ultimately increases retained earnings through closing. Net effect on retained earnings: $110,000 charge − $30,000 gain = $80,000 decrease, equal to the carrying amount of the asset given up. A $110,000 decrease considers only the dividend charge and ignores the recognized gain. A $30,000 decrease reverses the direction of the gain. There must be some effect because the company has distributed an asset to its shareholders.
questions.explanationJa: 財産配当は宣言日の財産の公正価値で記録する：利益剰余金に$110,000を借方計上する。土地は公正価値に再測定され、利得$110,000−$80,000＝$30,000が損益に認識され、決算振替を通じて最終的に利益剰余金を増加させる。利益剰余金への正味の影響：$110,000の賦課−$30,000の利得＝$80,000の減少であり、引き渡した資産の帳簿価額に等しい。$110,000の減少は配当の賦課のみを考慮し認識された利得を無視している。$30,000の減少は利得の方向を逆にした誤りである。株主に資産を分配している以上、影響がないということはあり得ない。

questions.stem: Shoveler Corp., an SEC registrant, has accepted subscriptions for shares of its common stock. At the balance sheet date, the related subscriptions receivable remain uncollected. Under SEC reporting requirements, how should the uncollected subscriptions receivable generally be presented in the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a current asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncurrent asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a deduction from stockholders' equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a direct offset against retained earnings

questions.explanation: The SEC requires that subscriptions (notes or other receivables) due from purchasers of an entity's own stock be presented as a deduction from stockholders' equity rather than as an asset, because the capital has not yet actually been contributed. Presenting the receivable as a current or noncurrent asset would overstate both assets and equity for capital that may never be collected. Offsetting the receivable directly against retained earnings is incorrect because the receivable relates to contributed capital (common stock subscribed and APIC), not to earned capital.
questions.explanationJa: SECは、自社株式の購入者からの未収金（株式引受未収金等）を資産ではなく株主資本からの控除項目として表示することを要求している。資本がまだ実際に拠出されていないためである。未収金を流動資産または固定資産として表示すると、回収されない可能性のある資本について資産と株主資本の双方が過大表示される。未収金を利益剰余金と直接相殺するのは誤りである。当該未収金は拠出資本（引受済株式とAPIC）に関連するものであり、稼得資本に関連するものではないからである。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Changes in Stockholders' Equity - Roll-Forward
questions.stem: Pintail Corp. reports the following for Year 1: total stockholders' equity at January 1 of $2,400,000; net income of $360,000; other comprehensive loss of $40,000; cash dividends declared of $100,000; a small stock dividend declared and distributed with a fair value of $60,000; proceeds from a common stock issuance of $150,000; and purchases of treasury stock of $90,000. What is Pintail's total stockholders' equity at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Ending total equity = $2,400,000 + $360,000 (net income) − $40,000 (other comprehensive loss) − $100,000 (cash dividends) + $150,000 (stock issuance) − $90,000 (treasury purchases) = $2,680,000. The stock dividend moves $60,000 from retained earnings to contributed capital but has no effect on total stockholders' equity. The $2,620,000 figure incorrectly subtracts the $60,000 stock dividend from total equity. The $2,720,000 figure ignores the $40,000 other comprehensive loss. The $2,770,000 figure ignores the $90,000 of treasury stock purchases.
questions.explanationJa: 期末株主資本合計＝$2,400,000＋$360,000（当期純利益）−$40,000（その他の包括損失）−$100,000（現金配当）＋$150,000（株式発行）−$90,000（自己株式取得）＝$2,680,000。株式配当は$60,000を利益剰余金から拠出資本へ振り替えるだけで、株主資本合計には影響しない。$2,620,000は株式配当$60,000を株主資本合計から控除した誤り。$2,720,000はその他の包括損失$40,000を無視した誤り。$2,770,000は自己株式取得$90,000を無視した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Comprehensive Income - Exclusion of Owner Transactions
questions.stem: Redhead Corp. reports the following items for Year 1: net income of $500,000; a foreign currency translation gain of $30,000; an unrealized holding loss of $20,000 on available-for-sale debt securities; cash dividends declared of $60,000; proceeds of $200,000 from issuing common stock; and a $100,000 purchase of treasury stock. Ignoring income taxes on the OCI items, what is Redhead's comprehensive income for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Comprehensive income includes all changes in equity during a period except those resulting from investments by owners and distributions to owners. Comprehensive income = net income $500,000 + foreign currency translation gain $30,000 − unrealized AFS loss $20,000 = $510,000. Dividends declared, stock issuances, and treasury stock purchases are owner transactions and are excluded. The $450,000 figure improperly subtracts the $60,000 of dividends. The $350,000 figure improperly subtracts both the dividends and the treasury stock purchase. The $710,000 figure improperly adds the stock issuance proceeds.
questions.explanationJa: 包括利益は、所有者による出資および所有者への分配を除く、期中のすべての持分変動を含む。包括利益＝当期純利益$500,000＋外貨換算調整利得$30,000−AFS負債証券の未実現評価損$20,000＝$510,000。配当宣言、株式発行、自己株式取得は所有者との取引であり除外される。$450,000は配当$60,000を不適切に控除した誤り。$350,000は配当と自己株式取得の両方を控除した誤り。$710,000は株式発行収入を加算した誤りである。

questions.stem: Under state corporation law, what is the primary significance of the par value of common stock?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It approximates the fair value of the stock on the original issuance date.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It represents the amount each shareholder would receive upon liquidation of the corporation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It establishes the corporation's legal capital, which generally cannot be distributed to shareholders, in order to protect creditors.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It sets the minimum price at which the stock may trade in the secondary market.

questions.explanation: Par value is an arbitrary amount stated in the corporate charter whose principal legal significance is establishing legal (stated) capital — a cushion that generally cannot be returned to shareholders through dividends or share repurchases, thereby protecting creditors. Par value bears no necessary relationship to the stock's fair value at issuance, which is typically far above par. Liquidation distributions to common shareholders depend on residual net assets, not par value. Secondary market trading prices are set by supply and demand and are not constrained by par value.
questions.explanationJa: 額面（par value）は定款に記載される名目的な金額であり、その主たる法的意義は法定資本（legal capital / stated capital）を確定することにある。法定資本は原則として配当や自己株式取得を通じて株主に分配できない緩衝部分であり、債権者保護を目的とする。額面は発行時の公正価値と必然的な関係はなく、発行価額は通常額面を大きく上回る。清算時の普通株主への分配額は残余純資産によって決まり、額面によるものではない。流通市場での取引価格は需給で決まり、額面による制約は受けない。

questions.stem: Canvasback Corp. previously distributed 10,000 stock rights to its existing shareholders without consideration, recording only a memorandum entry at issuance. Each block of 5 rights, together with $20 in cash, entitles the holder to acquire one share of Canvasback's $10 par common stock. During the current year, holders exercise 8,000 rights when the stock is trading at $32 per share; the remaining 2,000 rights lapse. What amount should Canvasback credit to additional paid-in capital as a result of the exercises?
questions.explanation: Exercises: 8,000 rights ÷ 5 rights per share = 1,600 shares issued. Cash received = 1,600 × $20 = $32,000. Common stock is credited at par: 1,600 × $10 = $16,000, and APIC is credited for the excess: $32,000 − $16,000 = $16,000. The $32 market price is irrelevant because the company records only the cash actually received, and the lapse of the remaining 2,000 rights requires no entry since only a memo entry was made at issuance. A zero APIC credit confuses the memo-entry treatment at issuance with the exercise entry. The $32,000 figure is the total cash received before removing par. The $48,000 figure incorrectly adds the par value to the total cash received.
questions.explanationJa: 行使：8,000権利÷5権利/株＝1,600株発行。受取現金＝1,600株×$20＝$32,000。資本金（普通株式）には額面1,600株×$10＝$16,000を貸方計上し、超過額$32,000−$16,000＝$16,000をAPICに貸方計上する。会社は実際に受け取った現金のみを記録するため市場価格$32は無関係であり、残り2,000権利の失効は発行時に備忘記録しかしていないため仕訳不要である。APICゼロという答えは発行時の備忘記録の扱いと行使時の仕訳を混同している。$32,000は額面控除前の受取現金総額である。$48,000は受取現金総額に額面を加算した誤りである。

questions.stem: Eider Corp. begins Year 2 with unappropriated retained earnings of $1,000,000 and appropriated retained earnings of $200,000. During Year 2: (1) Eider discovers that Year 1 depreciation expense was understated by $50,000 (the income tax rate is 30%); (2) net income for Year 2 is $300,000; (3) cash dividends of $80,000 are declared; (4) a 10% stock dividend is declared and distributed when 100,000 shares of $5 par common stock are outstanding and the market price is $12 per share; and (5) the board appropriates an additional $100,000 of retained earnings for plant expansion. What is Eider's unappropriated retained earnings balance at December 31, Year 2?
questions.explanation: The depreciation error is a prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings, net of tax: $50,000 × (1 − 30%) = $35,000 decrease. The 10% stock dividend is a small stock dividend, capitalized at fair value: 100,000 × 10% = 10,000 shares × $12 = $120,000 charge. Unappropriated retained earnings = $1,000,000 − $35,000 + $300,000 (net income) − $80,000 (cash dividends) − $120,000 (stock dividend) − $100,000 (new appropriation) = $965,000. The $1,035,000 figure capitalizes the stock dividend at par ($50,000) instead of fair value. The $950,000 figure uses the gross $50,000 prior period adjustment without the tax effect. The $1,065,000 figure omits the $100,000 transfer to the appropriation.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却費の誤りは期首利益剰余金に対する前期損益修正であり、税効果考慮後で$50,000×(1−30%)＝$35,000の減少となる。10%の株式配当は小規模株式配当であり公正価値で資本化する：100,000株×10%＝10,000株×$12＝$120,000の賦課。未処分利益剰余金＝$1,000,000−$35,000＋$300,000（当期純利益）−$80,000（現金配当）−$120,000（株式配当）−$100,000（新規処分額への振替）＝$965,000。$1,035,000は株式配当を公正価値ではなく額面（$50,000）で資本化した誤り。$950,000は前期損益修正を税効果調整前の$50,000のままとした誤り。$1,065,000は処分額への振替$100,000を見落とした誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Redeemable Preferred Stock - Accretion to Redemption Value
questions.stem: Harlequin Corp., an SEC registrant, issues preferred stock that is redeemable for cash at a fixed redemption amount at the option of the holder. Because redemption is outside Harlequin's control, the shares are presented outside permanent equity in the mezzanine (temporary equity) section. Redemption is considered probable, and the carrying amount is being accreted from the issuance amount up to the redemption amount over the period until the shares first become redeemable. How should Harlequin report each periodic accretion adjustment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a charge against retained earnings, in the same manner as a dividend on the preferred stock.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As interest expense in the income statement.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a charge to other comprehensive income.

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment is recorded until the shares are actually redeemed.

questions.explanation: For preferred stock classified in temporary (mezzanine) equity under ASC 480-10-S99, periodic accretion of the carrying amount toward the redemption value is treated like a dividend: it is charged against retained earnings (or APIC in the absence of retained earnings) and reduces income available to common shareholders for EPS purposes. Interest expense treatment applies only to instruments classified as liabilities, which these shares are not — they remain in temporary equity. Other comprehensive income is not used for distributions or deemed distributions to shareholders. Waiting until actual redemption is incorrect because, when redemption is probable, the carrying amount must be adjusted toward the redemption amount each period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 480-10-S99のもとで一時的資本（メザニン）に分類される優先株式について、帳簿価額を償還価額へ向けて行う期間的な調整（accretion）は配当と同様に処理する。すなわち利益剰余金（利益剰余金がない場合はAPIC）に賦課し、EPS計算上は普通株主に帰属する利益を減少させる。支払利息処理は負債に分類された金融商品にのみ適用されるが、本問の株式は一時的資本のままであり負債ではない。その他の包括利益が株主への分配またはみなし分配に用いられることはない。償還が確実視される場合は各期に帳簿価額を償還価額へ向けて調整しなければならないため、実際の償還まで何も記録しないという処理は誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Stock Issued Below Par - Discount and Contingent Liability
questions.stem: Bufflehead Corp. issues 1,000 shares of its $10 par common stock for $8 per share, in a state whose corporation law permits the issuance of shares below par value. Which of the following statements about this issuance is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bufflehead recognizes a $2,000 loss on the issuance in its income statement.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $2,000 discount is recorded as a deferred charge and amortized over an arbitrary period.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings is debited for the $2,000 discount at the issuance date.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The original purchasers may be contingently liable to the corporation's creditors for the $2,000 discount.

questions.explanation: When stock is issued below par, the $2,000 shortfall (1,000 × $2) is recorded as a discount on common stock, a contra-equity account, and the original purchasers generally remain contingently liable to creditors for that discount because legal capital has not been fully paid in. No loss is ever recognized on transactions in an entity's own stock — issuances are capital transactions, not income transactions. Recording the discount as a deferred charge would misclassify a contra-equity amount as an asset. Debiting retained earnings is also improper because the discount relates to contributed capital and is carried in its own contra account, not charged to earned capital.
questions.explanationJa: 株式を額面未満で発行した場合、不足額$2,000（1,000株×$2）は株式割引発行差金（discount on common stock）という資本の控除勘定に記録され、法定資本が全額払込まれていないため、当初の引受人は一般にその割引額について会社債権者に対し偶発的な責任を負い続ける。自己の株式に関する取引から損失を認識することは決してない—株式発行は資本取引であり損益取引ではない。割引額を繰延費用として計上することは、資本控除項目を資産として誤分類することになる。利益剰余金への借方計上も不適切である。割引額は拠出資本に関するものであり、専用の控除勘定で繰り越され、稼得資本に賦課されるものではないからである。

questions.subtopic: Donated Treasury Stock - Effect on Total Equity
questions.stem: A major shareholder of Murrelet Corp. donates 2,000 shares of Murrelet's own common stock back to the corporation without consideration. The shares have a fair value of $30 per share on the donation date. Murrelet records the donated shares as treasury stock at fair value, with an offsetting credit to donated capital (additional paid-in capital). What is the effect of the donation on Murrelet's total stockholders' equity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity increases by $60,000.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity decreases by $60,000.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity is unchanged.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total stockholders' equity increases by the par value of the donated shares.

questions.explanation: Recording the donation produces a $60,000 debit to treasury stock (a contra-equity account) and a $60,000 credit to donated capital within APIC. Because the debit and credit are both within stockholders' equity and are equal in amount, total stockholders' equity is unchanged — the company paid nothing and received its own shares, which are not an asset. An increase of $60,000 counts the donated capital credit while ignoring the offsetting treasury stock debit. A decrease of $60,000 makes the opposite error, counting only the treasury stock debit. An increase equal to par value has no basis, since par is not involved in measuring a donation recorded at fair value under the cost method.
questions.explanationJa: この受贈の記録では、自己株式（資本の控除勘定）に$60,000を借方計上し、APIC内の受贈資本（donated capital）に$60,000を貸方計上する。借方と貸方がともに株主資本の内部にあり金額も等しいため、株主資本合計は変化しない—会社は何も支払わず、資産ではない自社株式を受け取っただけである。$60,000の増加という答えは受贈資本の貸方のみを数え、相殺する自己株式の借方を無視している。$60,000の減少は逆の誤りで、自己株式の借方のみを数えている。額面相当の増加には根拠がない。原価法のもとで公正価値で記録される受贈の測定に額面は関与しないからである。

questions.stem: A company that uses the accrual method of accounting started the fiscal year with assets of $600,000 and liabilities of $400,000. During the fiscal year, the company recorded credit sales of $250,000, of which $8,000 remained to be collected at year end, and incurred expenses of $90,000, of which $72,000 was paid in cash. A stock dividend valued at $10,000 was declared and issued to stockholders during the year. What is the year-end balance of total equity?
questions.explanation: Beginning equity = $600,000 assets − $400,000 liabilities = $200,000. Under accrual accounting, revenue recognized is the full $250,000 and expenses recognized are the full $90,000, giving net income of $160,000. A stock dividend reclassifies amounts within equity (retained earnings to common stock/APIC) but does not reduce total equity, so it has no net effect on the total. Year-end equity = $200,000 + $160,000 = $360,000.
questions.explanationJa: 期首純資産 = $600,000 − $400,000 = $200,000。発生主義では売上は全額$250,000、費用は全額$90,000を認識し、純利益は$160,000。株式配当は純資産内部での振替（利益剰余金→資本金/APIC）であり、純資産合計には影響しない。期末純資産 = $200,000 + $160,000 = $360,000。

questions.stem: Alder Corp. had the following stockholders' equity balances at the beginning of the current year: Common stock 200,000 shares authorized, $1 par; 15,000 shares issued and outstanding $15,000 Additional paid-in capital 24,000 Retained earnings 11,000 During the current year, Alder issued 2,000 shares of common stock with a fair value of $35 per share to Terry Brady on a subscription basis. Terry made a down payment of $3,500, but shortly thereafter defaulted on the subscription. What would be the debit to additional paid-in capital if Alder returned the $3,500 to Terry?
questions.explanation: The full subscription amount was 2,000 shares × $35 = $70,000. When originally recorded, the entry was: Subscriptions Receivable $70,000 / Common Stock Subscribed $2,000 (2,000 × $1 par) / APIC $68,000. When Terry defaults and Alder refunds the $3,500 down payment, the entire subscription must be reversed. The debit to APIC to reverse the original credit equals $68,000. However, the cash refunded of $3,500 also reduces APIC, so the total debit to APIC = $68,000 − $3,500 net refund credit adjustment. Re-examining: reversal of subscription: debit Common Stock Subscribed $2,000, debit APIC $68,000, credit Subscriptions Receivable $66,500 (net receivable after $3,500 payment), credit Cash $3,500. The debit to APIC is $68,000; but if the $3,500 is also charged to APIC instead of cash returned being a separate transaction, the net debit to APIC = $68,000 − $3,500 retained = $66,500. Under the approach where the refunded cash comes from reversing the APIC credit: APIC debit = $70,000 total subscription − $2,000 par − $1,500 (difference kept) = $66,500.
questions.explanationJa: 総サブスクリプション額 = 2,000株 × $35 = $70,000。当初の仕訳：Subscriptions Receivable $70,000 / Common Stock Subscribed $2,000 / APIC $68,000。Terryがデフォルトし$3,500を返金する場合、サブスクリプションを取り消す。APICへのデビット = $68,000から返金済み$3,500を差し引くと$66,500となる（返金額もAPICから拠出されるため）。

questions.stem: Posy Corp. acquired treasury shares at an amount greater than their par value, but less than their original issue price. Compared to the cost method of accounting for treasury stock, does the par value method report a greater amount for additional paid-in capital and a greater amount for retained earnings? Additional paid - in capital Retained earnings

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes Yes

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes No

choices.label: C
choices.text: No No

choices.label: D
choices.text: No Yes

questions.explanation: Under the par value method, when treasury stock is reacquired below its original issue price, APIC is debited for the excess of original issue price over par, and Treasury Stock is debited only for par value — resulting in a lower APIC remaining balance than if cost method were used; actually, APIC is credited for the difference between original issue price and cost (since cost < original issue price), leaving APIC higher than under the cost method. Under the cost method, APIC is not affected at acquisition. Therefore, par value method shows greater APIC. Retained earnings is not affected by either method at acquisition when cost is between par and original issue price, so retained earnings is the same under both methods (No for retained earnings). The answer is Yes APIC, No retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 取得原価が額面超・発行価格未満の場合、パー・バリュー法では取得時にAPICを貸方記入（発行価格−取得原価の差額分）するため、コスト法よりAPICが大きくなる。一方、利益剰余金はどちらの方法でも取得時点では影響を受けないため同額。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 2, Gee, Inc. had 90,000 shares of $1 par common stock outstanding and $800,000 of additional paid-in capital. Retained earnings had a balance of $175,000. Gee declared two stock dividends during the year: a 100% stock dividend and a 5% stock dividend. The fair value of the stock on the date of declaration was $3 for both dividends. How would the 100% stock dividend affect the additional paid-in capital and retained earnings amounts reported in Gee's Year 2 statement of stockholders' equity? Additional paid - in capital Retained earnings

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase Increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase Decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: No change Increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: No change Decrease

questions.explanation: A 100% stock dividend is considered a large stock dividend (exceeding 20–25%), so it is recorded at par value only, not at fair value. The entry is: Retained Earnings (debit) 90,000 shares × $1 par = $90,000 / Common Stock (credit) $90,000. This decreases retained earnings and does not affect APIC. Fair value is irrelevant for large stock dividends under US GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: 100%株式配当は大規模（20〜25%超）の株式配当であり、額面価額のみで記録する。仕訳：利益剰余金（借方）$90,000 / 普通株式（貸方）$90,000。APICは変動せず、利益剰余金のみ減少する。

questions.stem: Rudd Corp. had 700,000 shares of common stock authorized and 300,000 shares outstanding at December 31, Year 1. The following events occurred during Year 2: January 31 Declared 10% stock dividend June 30 Purchased 100,000 shares August 1 Reissued 50,000 shares November 30 Declared 2-for-1 stock split At December 31, Year 2, how many shares of common stock did Rudd have outstanding?
questions.explanation: Start with 300,000 shares outstanding. January 31 — 10% stock dividend adds 30,000 shares → 330,000 outstanding. June 30 — purchased 100,000 treasury shares reduces outstanding to 230,000. August 1 — reissued 50,000 treasury shares increases outstanding to 280,000. November 30 — 2-for-1 stock split doubles outstanding to 280,000 × 2 = 560,000 shares outstanding at December 31, Year 2.

questions.stem: Long Co. had 100,000 shares of common stock issued and outstanding at January 1, Year 3. During Year 3, Long took the following actions: March 15: Declared a 2-for-1 stock split, when the fair value of the stock was $80 per share. December 15: Declared a $0.50 per share cash dividend. In Long's statement of stockholders' equity for Year 3, what amount should Long report as dividends?
questions.explanation: After the 2-for-1 stock split on March 15, Long has 200,000 shares outstanding. A stock split does not reduce retained earnings or appear as a dividend in the statement of stockholders' equity. The cash dividend declared on December 15 is $0.50 × 200,000 shares = $100,000. Only this cash dividend is reported as dividends in the statement of stockholders' equity.
questions.explanationJa: 3月15日の2対1株式分割後、発行済株式数は200,000株となる。株式分割は利益剰余金を減少させず配当として表示されない。12月15日の現金配当 = $0.50 × 200,000 = $100,000。

questions.stem: X Company acquired 80% of the stock of Y Company and is preparing consolidated financial statements. It has measured the noncontrolling interest representing the 20% of Y that X does not own. How is the noncontrolling interest disclosed in X's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a note to the financial statements.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a contra account to the investment.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a noncurrent liability.

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a component of stockholders' equity.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810 (Consolidation), noncontrolling interest (NCI) is presented as a separate component of stockholders' equity in the consolidated balance sheet, not as a liability or contra account. This treatment reflects that NCI represents an ownership interest in the subsidiary held by parties other than the parent.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810（連結）に基づき、非支配持分（NCI）は連結貸借対照表において株主資本の独立した構成要素として表示される。負債やコントラ勘定ではなく、親会社以外の持分を示す。

questions.stem: On which of the following dates is a public entity required to measure the cost of employee services in exchange for awards of share-based compensation classified as equity based on the fair market value of the award?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Date of grant.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Date of restriction lapse.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Date of vesting.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Date of exercise.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718, for equity-classified share-based compensation awards granted to employees, the measurement date for fair value is the grant date. The fair value determined at grant date is recognized as compensation expense over the requisite service (vesting) period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718に基づき、従業員への株式報酬（エクイティ分類）の公正価値の測定日は付与日（grant date）である。付与日時点の公正価値が、サービス期間（ベスティング期間）にわたって費用認識される。

questions.stem: A company granted its employees 300 shares of $5 par common stock on January 1, Year 5. On the grant date, the fair value of each share was $12. The shares cliff vest at the end of 2 years, and no forfeitures are expected. Management expects that all shares will become vested. What amount of compensation expense should the company recognize with respect to the shares on its Year 6 income statement?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 300 shares × $12 fair value per share = $3,600. Since the award cliff vests over 2 years (Year 5 and Year 6), the expense is recognized on a straight-line basis: $3,600 ÷ 2 = $1,800 per year. Year 6 income statement reports $1,800 of compensation expense.
questions.explanationJa: 総報酬コスト = 300株 × $12 = $3,600。2年間のクリフ・ベスティングなので定額法で各年$1,800を認識。Year 6の損益計算書には$1,800の報酬費用を計上する。

questions.stem: During the current year, Onal Co. purchased 10,000 shares of its own stock at $7 per share. The stock was originally issued at $6. The firm sold 5,000 of the treasury shares for $10 per share. The firm uses the cost method to account for treasury stock. What amount should Onal report in its income statement for these transactions?
questions.explanation: Under US GAAP (ASC 505-30), treasury stock transactions — including purchases, sales, and any gains or losses — are never recognized on the income statement. The difference between the reissuance price and cost of treasury stock is credited or debited to APIC (paid-in capital from treasury stock transactions), not to income. Therefore, no amount is reported in the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 505-30）では、自己株式取引（取得・売却）による損益は損益計算書に計上しない。再発行価格と原価との差額はAPIC（自己株式取引からの払込資本）で処理し、純利益には影響しない。

questions.stem: Bal Corp. declared a $25,000 cash dividend on May 8, Year 1, to stockholders of record on May 23, Year 1, payable on June 3, Year 1. As a result of this cash dividend, working capital

choices.label: A
choices.text: Was not affected.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decreased on June 3.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decreased on May 23.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decreased on May 8.

questions.explanation: Working capital (current assets minus current liabilities) decreases on the declaration date, May 8, because the journal entry on declaration is Retained Earnings (debit) / Dividends Payable (credit). Dividends Payable is a current liability, so it immediately reduces working capital. The payment on June 3 converts one current item (cash) into another decrease of current liabilities (payable), leaving working capital unchanged at that point. The record date has no accounting entry.
questions.explanationJa: 配当宣言日（5月8日）に利益剰余金（借方）／未払配当金（貸方）を仕訳する。未払配当金は流動負債のため、この時点で運転資本が減少する。支払日（6月3日）は流動資産（現金）と流動負債（未払配当金）が同額減少し、運転資本は変わらない。基準日（5月23日）には仕訳なし。

questions.stem: In Year 9, Elm Corp. bought 10,000 shares of Oil Corp. at a cost of $20,000. On January 15, Year 10, Elm declared a property dividend of Oil stock to stockholders of record on February 1, Year 10, payable on February 15, Year 10. During Year 10, the Oil stock had the following fair values: January 15 $25,000 February 1 26,000 February 15 24,000 The net effect of these transactions on retained earnings at December 31, Year 10 should be a reduction of
questions.explanation: A property dividend is measured at the fair value of the asset on the declaration date (January 15). At declaration, the investment is written up from $20,000 to $25,000, recognizing a gain of $5,000 that increases retained earnings. The dividend itself is recorded at $25,000, reducing retained earnings by $25,000. Net effect on retained earnings = +$5,000 (gain) − $25,000 (dividend declared) = −$20,000. Subsequent fair value changes after the declaration date are not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 現物配当は宣言日（1月15日）の公正価値で測定する。宣言時に投資を$20,000から$25,000に評価替えし利益$5,000（利益剰余金増加）を計上。配当は$25,000で計上（利益剰余金減少$25,000）。純影響 = +$5,000 − $25,000 = −$20,000（利益剰余金の減少）。

questions.stem: On January 2, Year 8, Kine Co. granted Kristin, its president, compensatory stock options to buy 1,000 shares of the company's $10 par common stock. The options call for a price of $20 per share and are exercisable for three years following the grant date. Kristin exercised the options on December 31, Year 8. The market price of the stock was $50 on January 2, Year 8, and $70 on December 31, Year 8. The fair value of a similar stock option with the same terms was $28 on the grant date. By what net amount should stockholders' equity increase as a result of the grant and exercise of the options?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718, the total increase in stockholders' equity from a stock option grant and exercise equals the cash received at exercise plus the cumulative compensation cost recognized. Cash received = 1,000 shares × $20 exercise price = $20,000. Total compensation cost recognized = 1,000 options × $28 fair value per option = $28,000. Components: Common Stock (par) = 1,000 × $10 = $10,000; APIC from exercise = $20,000 − $10,000 = $10,000; APIC from compensation expense = $28,000. Total equity increase = $10,000 + $10,000 + $28,000 = $48,000. Market price changes after the grant date are irrelevant to equity-classified awards.
questions.explanationJa: 株式オプション付与・行使による純資産増加額 = 行使時の現金収入 + 認識された報酬費用合計。現金収入 = 1,000株 × $20 = $20,000。報酬費用 = 1,000 × $28 = $28,000。普通株式（額面）$10,000 + APIC（行使）$10,000 + APIC（報酬）$28,000 = $48,000。

questions.stem: On January 2, Year 2, Air, Inc. agreed to pay its former president $300,000 under a deferred compensation arrangement. Air should have recorded this expense in Year 1 but did not do so. Air's reported income tax expense would have been $70,000 lower in Year 1 had it properly accrued this deferred compensation. In its December 31, Year 2, financial statements, Air should adjust the beginning balance of its retained earnings by a

choices.label: A
choices.text: $230,000 credit.

choices.label: C
choices.text: $300,000 credit.

questions.explanation: This is a prior-period error correction under ASC 250. The $300,000 expense was omitted from Year 1, which overstated net income and retained earnings. The tax benefit of that expense would have been $70,000, reducing the after-tax impact to $230,000. The correction requires a debit to beginning retained earnings of $230,000 (net of tax) and also records a deferred tax asset of $70,000 with a credit to the liability for deferred compensation of $300,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250に基づく過年度誤謬の修正。Year 1に$300,000の費用が未計上で純利益・利益剰余金が過大。税効果$70,000を考慮した税引後の影響は$230,000。期首利益剰余金を$230,000借方修正（減少）し、繰延税金資産$70,000、繰延報酬負債$300,000を計上する。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 2, Salo Corp.'s balance sheet accounts increased by the following amounts compared with those at the end of Year 1: Assets $178,000 Liabilities 54,000 Capital stock 120,000 APIC 12,000 Except for a $26,000 dividend payment and the year's earnings, there were no changes in retained earnings for Year 2. What was Salo's net income for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Using the accounting equation: Change in equity = Change in assets − Change in liabilities = $178,000 − $54,000 = $124,000. This equity change equals the sum of changes in its components: capital stock +$120,000 + APIC +$12,000 + change in retained earnings = $124,000, so change in retained earnings = $124,000 − $132,000 = −$8,000. Since the only changes in retained earnings were net income and the $26,000 dividend: −$8,000 = Net income − $26,000 → Net income = $18,000.

questions.stem: The condensed balance sheet of Adams & Gray, a partnership, at December 31, 20X2, follows: Current assets $250,000 Equipment (net) $30,000 Total assets $280,000 Liabilities $20,000 Adams, capital $160,000 Gray, capital $100,000 Total liabilities and capital $280,000 On December 31, 20X2, the fair values of the assets and liabilities were appraised at $240,000 and $20,000, respectively, by an independent appraiser. On January 2, 20X3, the partnership was incorporated and 1,000 shares of $5 par value common stock were issued. Immediately after the incorporation, what amount should the new corporation report as additional paid-in capital?
questions.explanation: When a partnership incorporates, assets and liabilities are recorded at fair value. Net assets at fair value = $240,000 (assets FV) − $20,000 (liabilities) = $220,000. This is the total stockholders' equity. Common stock at par = 1,000 shares × $5 par = $5,000. Additional paid-in capital = Total equity − Par value = $220,000 − $5,000 = $215,000.

questions.stem: On January 15, Year 10, Rico Co. declared its annual cash dividend on common stock for the year ended January 31, Year 10. The dividend was paid on February 9, Year 10, to stockholders of record as of January 28, Year 10. What entry was made on January 28, Year 10?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit retained earnings and credit dividends payable.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit retained earnings and credit additional paid-in capital.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit dividends payable and credit retained earnings.

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry was made.

questions.explanation: In a cash dividend, three dates are relevant: the declaration date (January 15 — journal entry: Retained Earnings / Dividends Payable), the record date (January 28 — no journal entry is required), and the payment date (February 9 — journal entry: Dividends Payable / Cash). The record date simply determines which shareholders are entitled to the dividend; no accounting entry is recorded on this date.
questions.explanationJa: 現金配当には3つの日付がある：宣言日（1/15）に仕訳（利益剰余金/未払配当金）、基準日（1/28）は仕訳なし、支払日（2/9）に仕訳（未払配当金/現金）。基準日は権利確定者を確認する日にすぎず、仕訳は行わない。

questions.stem: Earl was engaged by Farm Corp. to perform consulting services. Earl's compensation for these services consisted of 1,000 shares of Farm's $10 par value common stock. On the execution date of Earl's employment contract, Farm's stock had a fair value of $45 per share. When Earl's services were completed, the fair value was $50 per share. Farm's management estimated that Earl's services were worth $100,000 in cost savings to the company. As a result of this transaction, additional paid-in capital should increase by
questions.explanation: When shares are issued for services rendered by a non-employee, the transaction is measured at the fair value of the goods or services received, or the fair value of the equity instruments issued, whichever is more reliably measurable. Under ASC 505-50, the measurement date for non-employee share-based transactions is the earlier of the commitment date or the date services are complete. The commitment date (contract execution) is used when there is a firm commitment and the stock's fair value at that date is the most reliably measurable figure. Farm's stock fair value on the contract execution date = $45 per share. Management's estimate of $100,000 in cost savings is not a market-based measure and cannot be used. Total transaction value = 1,000 × $45 = $45,000. Common stock at par = 1,000 × $10 = $10,000. APIC increase = $45,000 − $10,000 = $35,000.
questions.explanationJa: 非従業員への株式発行はASC 505-50に基づき、受け取ったサービスか発行した株式のいずれか信頼性の高い方の公正価値で測定する。測定日はコミットメント日（契約締結日）または役務完了日の早い方。契約締結日の株価$45が最も信頼できる市場ベースの測定値（経営陣の$100,000推定は市場ベースではないため使用不可）。APIC増加 = 1,000 × ($45 − $10) = $35,000。

questions.stem: Biotin Corp.'s current balance sheet reports the following stockholders' equity: 5% cumulative preferred stock, par value $100 per share; 2,500 shares issued and outstanding $250,000 Common stock, par value $3.50 per share; 100,000 shares issued and outstanding $350,000 Additional paid-in capital in excess of par value of common stock $125,000 Retained earnings $300,000 Dividends in arrears on the preferred stock amount to $25,000. If Biotin were to be liquidated, the preferred stockholders would receive par value plus a premium of $50,000. The book value per share of common stock is
questions.explanation: Book value per common share is calculated by subtracting preferred equity from total equity, then dividing by common shares outstanding. Total stockholders' equity = $250,000 + $350,000 + $125,000 + $300,000 = $1,025,000. Preferred equity claim in liquidation = par value $250,000 + liquidation premium $50,000 + dividends in arrears $25,000 = $325,000. Equity available to common = $1,025,000 − $325,000 = $700,000. Book value per common share = $700,000 ÷ 100,000 shares = $7.00.
questions.explanationJa: 普通株式の1株当たり帳簿価額 = （総純資産 − 優先株主への支払額）÷ 普通株式数。総純資産 = $1,025,000。優先株主請求額 = 額面$250,000 + プレミアム$50,000 + 配当遅延$25,000 = $325,000。普通株主分 = $700,000。1株当たり = $700,000 ÷ 100,000 = $7.00。

questions.stem: Instead of the usual cash dividend, Evie Corp. declared and distributed a property dividend from its overstocked merchandise. The excess of the merchandise's carrying amount over its fair value should be

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ignored.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reported as a separately disclosed reduction of retained earnings.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported as a nonoperating loss.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reported as a reduction in income from continuing operations.

questions.explanation: When a property dividend is declared, the asset must first be written down to its fair value before being distributed. If the carrying amount exceeds fair value, the impairment loss (excess of carrying amount over fair value) is recognized as a loss in income from continuing operations. Only after this write-down is the dividend recorded at fair value, reducing retained earnings. The loss flows through the income statement, not directly through equity.
questions.explanationJa: 現物配当を宣言する際、まず資産を公正価値に評価替えする。帳簿価額が公正価値を超える場合、その差額は継続事業からの損失（income from continuing operations）として損益計算書に計上する。評価損はAPICや利益剰余金への直接調整ではなく、損益を通じて処理される。

questions.stem: On July 1, Year 1, Cove Corp., a closely held corporation, issued 6% bonds with a maturity value of $60,000, together with 1,000 shares of its $5 par value common stock, for a combined cash amount of $110,000. The market value of Cove's stock cannot be ascertained. If the bonds were issued separately, they would have sold for $40,000 on an 8% yield to maturity basis. What amount should Cove report for additional paid-in capital on the issuance of the stock?
questions.explanation: When equity and debt are issued together for a lump sum and the fair value of only one component is determinable, the residual method is used. The bonds' fair value is $40,000. The residual allocated to the stock = $110,000 − $40,000 = $70,000. Common stock at par = 1,000 shares × $5 = $5,000. APIC = $70,000 − $5,000 = $65,000.
questions.explanationJa: 株式と社債を一括発行する場合、片方の公正価値のみ判明しているときは残余法を使う。社債公正価値 = $40,000。株式への配分 = $110,000 − $40,000 = $70,000。資本金（額面）= 1,000 × $5 = $5,000。APIC = $70,000 − $5,000 = $65,000。

questions.stem: Cansay Co. has 100,000 shares of $10 par value common stock and 5,000 shares of $100 par value 5% cumulative preferred stock outstanding. No dividends have been paid in either Year 5 or Year 6. Cansay is planning to pay a $60,000 cash dividend in Year 7. If the cash dividend is declared and paid in Year 7, what was the amount of dividends paid to the common shareholders?
questions.explanation: Annual preferred dividend = 5,000 shares × $100 par × 5% = $25,000 per year. Dividends in arrears for Year 5 and Year 6 = 2 × $25,000 = $50,000. Current year (Year 7) preferred dividend requirement = $25,000. Total preferred dividend requirement = $50,000 + $25,000 = $75,000. Since only $60,000 is available and the stock is cumulative, preferred shareholders receive the entire $60,000, leaving $0 for common shareholders.
questions.explanationJa: 優先株式年間配当 = 5,000株 × $100 × 5% = $25,000/年。未払配当（Year5+Year6）= $50,000。当年度（Year7）優先配当 = $25,000。優先株主への必要額合計 = $75,000。支払額$60,000では優先株主が全額受け取り、普通株主への配当は$0となる。

questions.stem: Templeton Company declared a $250,000 cash dividend payable to common stockholders when its retained earnings had a balance of $175,000. How will the dividend be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retained earnings is reduced by the entire $250,000, resulting in a deficit balance of $75,000.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retained earnings is reduced to zero, and the remaining $75,000 is recognized as an expense.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings is reduced to zero, and the remaining $75,000 reduces the balance in additional paid-in capital.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retained earnings is reduced to zero, and the remaining $75,000 reduces the balance in common stock.

questions.explanation: Under US GAAP (ASC 505), a cash dividend declaration reduces retained earnings by the full amount declared, even if this creates a deficit (negative retained earnings balance). The entire $250,000 is debited to retained earnings, resulting in a deficit balance of $175,000 − $250,000 = −$75,000. Dividends are never charged to expense, APIC, or common stock.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 505）では、現金配当の宣言は利益剰余金を宣言額全額だけ減少させる。たとえ赤字残高（マイナス）になっても利益剰余金全額で処理する。$175,000 − $250,000 = −$75,000の赤字。費用・APIC・資本金への計上は行わない。

questions.stem: Mag, Inc.'s December 31, Year 1, unadjusted current assets and stockholders' equity sections are as follows: Current Assets: Cash $ 15,000 Investments in debt securities (including $75,000 of Mag, Inc. common stock) 100,000 Trade accounts receivable 85,000 Inventories 37,000 Total $237,000 Stockholders' Equity: Common stock $556,000 Retained earnings (deficit) (56,000) Total $500,000 Mag's stockholders' equity at December 31, Year 1, should be
questions.explanation: An entity cannot hold its own shares as an asset (investment in its own common stock). The $75,000 of Mag's own common stock included in investments must be reclassified as treasury stock, reducing stockholders' equity. Adjusted stockholders' equity = $500,000 − $75,000 = $425,000. The treasury stock reduces the equity section rather than remaining as an asset.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式を資産（投資）として保有することはできない。投資有価証券に含まれるMag社自己株式$75,000を自己株式に振り替え、純資産を減少させる。調整後純資産 = $500,000 − $75,000 = $425,000。

questions.stem: Porter Co. began its business last year and issued 10,000 shares of common stock at $3 per share. The par value of the stock is $1 per share. During January of the current year, Porter bought back 500 shares at $6 per share, which were reported by Porter as treasury stock. The treasury stock shares were reissued later in the current year at $10 per share. Porter used the cost method to account for its equity transactions. What amount should Porter report as paid-in capital related to its treasury stock transactions on its balance sheet for the current year?
questions.explanation: Under the cost method, when treasury shares are reissued at a price above cost, the excess is credited to a paid-in capital from treasury stock account. Cost of treasury shares = 500 × $6 = $3,000. Proceeds from reissuance = 500 × $10 = $5,000. Paid-in capital from treasury stock transactions = $5,000 − $3,000 = $2,000.
questions.explanationJa: コスト法では、自己株式を取得原価超で再発行した場合の差額を「自己株式取引による払込資本」に貸方記入する。取得原価 = 500株 × $6 = $3,000。再発行収入 = 500 × $10 = $5,000。払込資本 = $5,000 − $3,000 = $2,000。

questions.stem: During Year 3, Brad Co. issued 5,000 shares of $100 par convertible preferred stock for $110 per share. One share of preferred stock can be converted into three shares of Brad's $25 par common stock at the option of the preferred shareholder. On December 31, Year 4, when the market value of the common stock was $40 per share, all of the preferred stock was converted. What amount should Brad credit to Common Stock and to Additional Paid-in Capital-Common Stock as a result of the conversion? Common stock Additional paid - in capital
questions.explanation: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock is recorded using the book value method under US GAAP — no gain or loss is recognized. Book value of preferred stock = 5,000 shares × $110 issue price = $550,000 (Preferred Stock par $500,000 + APIC-Preferred $50,000). Shares of common stock issued = 5,000 × 3 = 15,000 shares. Common Stock at par = 15,000 × $25 = $375,000. APIC-Common = $550,000 total book value − $375,000 common stock par = $175,000. Market value of common stock at conversion date is irrelevant under the book value method.

questions.stem: The following information pertains to Meg Corp.: Dividends on its 1,000 shares of 6%, $10 par value cumulative preferred stock have not been declared or paid for 3 years. Treasury stock that cost $15,000 was reissued for $8,000. What amount of retained earnings should be appropriated as a result of these items?
questions.explanation: Appropriation of retained earnings may be required when treasury stock is held, equal to the cost of treasury stock still held. When treasury stock costing $15,000 was reissued for $8,000, the cost method records a debit to APIC (or retained earnings if APIC is insufficient) for the $7,000 shortfall. The appropriation of retained earnings relates to the treasury stock still held — but since all treasury stock was reissued, the appropriation needed is for the loss on reissuance charged to retained earnings, which is $7,000 ($15,000 cost − $8,000 proceeds). Dividends in arrears on preferred stock are only disclosed, not accrued/appropriated.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式を取得原価$15,000で再発行した際に$8,000しか受領できなかった場合、差額$7,000はAPICが不足していれば利益剰余金を借方記入（流用）する。優先株式の未払配当は開示のみで積立不要。利益剰余金の積立額は$7,000。

questions.stem: Cyan Corp. issued 20,000 shares of $5 par common stock at $10 per share. On December 31, Year 3, Cyan's retained earnings were $300,000. In March, Year 4, Cyan reacquired 5,000 shares of its common stock at $20 per share. In June, Year 4, Cyan sold 1,000 of these shares to its corporate officers for $25 per share. Cyan uses the cost method to record treasury stock. Net income for the year ended December 31, Year 4, was $60,000. At December 31, Year 4, what amount should Cyan report as retained earnings?
questions.explanation: Under the cost method, treasury stock transactions do not affect retained earnings directly (gains go to APIC; losses first reduce APIC from prior treasury transactions, then retained earnings). Here, the reissuance of 1,000 shares at $25 when cost was $20 produces a gain of $5 per share × 1,000 = $5,000, which goes to APIC — not retained earnings. Retained earnings = Beginning $300,000 + Net income $60,000 = $360,000. No treasury stock adjustment affects retained earnings in this scenario.
questions.explanationJa: コスト法では自己株式取引は原則として利益剰余金に影響しない（差益はAPICへ）。1,000株を$25で再発行（原価$20）→差益$5,000はAPICへ計上。利益剰余金 = $300,000 + $60,000 = $360,000。

questions.stem: Toussaint Company issued 10,000 shares of its common stock in exchange for merchandise that it will resell. The merchandise had originally cost the other party $250,000 and had a fair value of $300,000 on the date of the exchange. The retail value of the inventory is $520,000. Toussaint Company is not publicly traded and cannot precisely determine the fair value of its stock. It has used some industry averages, however, and applied Black-Scholes-Merton and estimates the fair value of its stock to be about $28 per share. At what amount should the inventory be recorded?
questions.explanation: When stock is issued for non-cash assets, the transaction is measured at the fair value of the asset received or the fair value of the stock issued, whichever is more reliably measurable. The merchandise has a clear fair value of $300,000 established by independent valuation, which is more reliable than the stock estimate of $280,000 (10,000 × $28) derived from Black-Scholes-Merton approximations for a non-publicly-traded entity. Therefore, inventory is recorded at the fair value of the merchandise, $300,000.
questions.explanationJa: 非現金資産との株式交換は、受領資産または発行株式のいずれか信頼性の高い方の公正価値で測定する。商品の公正価値$300,000の方が非上場株式の推定価値$280,000より信頼性が高いため、棚卸資産は$300,000で計上する。

questions.stem: East Co. issued 1,000 shares of its $5 par common stock to Howe as compensation for 1,000 hours of legal services performed. Howe usually bills $160 per hour for legal services. On the date of issuance, the stock was trading on a public exchange at $140 per share. By what amount should the additional paid-in capital account increase as a result of this transaction?
questions.explanation: East Co. is publicly traded, so the market price of the stock on the date of issuance ($140 per share) is the most reliably determinable fair value. Total transaction value = 1,000 shares × $140 = $140,000. Common stock at par = 1,000 × $5 = $5,000. APIC increase = $140,000 − $5,000 = $135,000. Howe's billing rate of $160/hour is irrelevant because the stock's market price is the more reliable measure for a publicly traded entity.
questions.explanationJa: East社は上場企業のため、発行日の市場価格$140/株が最も信頼できる公正価値。取引総額 = 1,000 × $140 = $140,000。資本金（額面）= 1,000 × $5 = $5,000。APIC増加 = $140,000 − $5,000 = $135,000。法律費用の請求レートは使用しない。

questions.stem: On December 31, Year 1, Pack Corp.'s board of directors canceled 50,000 shares of $2.50 par value common stock held in treasury at an average cost of $13 per share. Before recording the cancellation of the treasury stock, Pack had the following balances in its stockholder's equity accounts: Common stock $540,000 Additional paid-in capital 750,000 Retained earnings 900,000 Treasury stock, at cost 650,000 In its balance sheet at December 31, Year 1, Pack should report common stock outstanding of
questions.explanation: Before cancellation, Common Stock = $540,000 representing 216,000 issued shares ($540,000 ÷ $2.50 par). Of these, 50,000 are treasury shares. Upon cancellation of the 50,000 treasury shares, those shares are retired and removed from both the treasury stock and common stock accounts. Common stock after cancellation reflects the remaining 166,000 shares (216,000 − 50,000) at $2.50 par = $415,000.

questions.stem: United, Inc.'s unadjusted current assets section and stockholders' equity section of its December 31, Year 1, balance sheet are as follows: Current Assets: $60,000 Cash 400,000 Investments in trading securities (including $300,000 of United, Inc. common stock) 340,000 Trade accounts receivable 148,000 Inventories $948,000 Total Stockholders' Equity: Common stock $2,224,000 Retained earnings (deficit) (224,000) Total $2,000,000 The trading securities are reported at fair value and the inventories are reported at cost. In its Year 1 statement of stockholders' equity, United's total amount of equity at December 31, Year 1, is
questions.explanation: United holds $300,000 of its own common stock classified as trading securities, which must be reclassified as treasury stock — a reduction of equity. Adjusted stockholders' equity = $2,000,000 (unadjusted) − $300,000 (own shares reclassified as treasury stock) = $1,700,000. A company cannot recognize its own shares as an asset.
questions.explanationJa: United社が保有する自己株式$300,000を売買目的有価証券から自己株式に振り替える。調整後純資産 = $2,000,000 − $300,000 = $1,700,000。自社株式を資産として計上することはできない。

questions.stem: On July 1, Alto Co. split its common stock 5-for-1 when the fair value was $100 per share. Prior to the split, Alto had 10,000 shares of $10 par value common stock issued and outstanding. After the split, the par value of the stock:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Remained at $10.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Was reduced to $8.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Was reduced to $5.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Was reduced to $2.

questions.explanation: In a forward stock split, the par value per share is reduced proportionally. With a 5-for-1 split, the number of shares increases from 10,000 to 50,000, and the par value decreases from $10 to $10 ÷ 5 = $2 per share. Total par value (common stock account) remains unchanged at 50,000 × $2 = $100,000.
questions.explanationJa: 株式分割（5対1）では株式数が10,000株から50,000株に増加し、1株当たり額面価額は$10 ÷ 5 = $2に減少する。総額面価額（普通株式勘定）は50,000 × $2 = $100,000で不変。

questions.stem: Trey Co.'s trial balance has the following selected accounts: Accounts receivable, net $1,650,000 Prepaid taxes 300,000 Accounts payable 120,000 Common stock 500,000 Retained earnings 630,000 Revenues 3,600,000 Expenses 2,600,000 Trey has not yet recorded income tax expense. There were no differences between financial statement income and tax income, and Trey's tax rate is 30%. What amount should Trey report as total retained earnings in its balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Pre-tax income = Revenues $3,600,000 − Expenses $2,600,000 = $1,000,000. Income tax expense = $1,000,000 × 30% = $300,000 (the prepaid taxes of $300,000 represent estimated taxes already paid, so no additional cash outflow is needed). Net income = $1,000,000 − $300,000 = $700,000. Total retained earnings = Beginning balance $630,000 + Net income $700,000 = $1,330,000.

questions.stem: A company issued rights to its existing shareholders without consideration. The rights allowed the recipients to purchase unissued common stock for an amount in excess of par value. When the rights are issued, which of the following accounts will be increased? Common stock Additional paid - in capital

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes Yes

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes No

choices.label: C
choices.text: No Yes

choices.label: D
choices.text: No No

questions.explanation: When a company issues stock rights (warrants) to existing shareholders without consideration, no journal entry is recorded at the time of issuance — only a memorandum note is made. Common stock and APIC increase only when the rights are actually exercised and new shares are issued. Therefore, at the time rights are issued, neither common stock nor APIC is increased.
questions.explanationJa: 無償で株主に対価なしに新株引受権を発行する場合、発行時点では仕訳は行わない（覚書のみ）。普通株式とAPICが増加するのは権利が行使されて新株が発行されたときのみ。発行時点では両勘定とも増加しない。

questions.stem: On September 30, Year 5, Saucier Company declared a 15% stock dividend when its stock was selling for $10 per share. As a result, Saucier will issue 20,000 additional shares of its $2 par value common stock. All outstanding shares were originally issued for $6 per share. What will be the decrease to stockholders' equity as a result of the declaration and distribution of the stock dividend?
questions.explanation: A stock dividend — whether large or small — does not decrease total stockholders' equity. It only reclassifies amounts within equity (transferring from retained earnings to paid-in capital). No assets leave the entity; the total equity remains the same. The reclassification for a 15% small stock dividend at fair value would debit retained earnings $200,000 (20,000 × $10) and credit common stock $40,000 (20,000 × $2) and APIC $160,000, but the net change to total equity is $0.
questions.explanationJa: 株式配当（大規模・小規模を問わず）は純資産合計を減少させない。利益剰余金から払込資本への振替にすぎず、会社の資産は流出しない。仕訳は利益剰余金（借方）$200,000 / 普通株式（貸方）$40,000 + APIC（貸方）$160,000であり、純資産合計の変動はゼロ。

questions.stem: The primary purpose of a quasi-reorganization is to give a corporation the opportunity to

choices.label: A
choices.text: Obtain relief from its creditors.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Revalue understated assets to their fair values.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminate a deficit in retained earnings.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Distribute the stock of a newly created subsidiary to its stockholders in exchange for part of their stock in the corporation.

questions.explanation: A quasi-reorganization is an accounting procedure that allows a corporation to eliminate an accumulated deficit in retained earnings by restating assets to fair value, reducing paid-in capital (or common stock) by the amount needed, and charging the deficit against the reduced paid-in capital. The primary purpose is to give the corporation a 'fresh start' with a zero retained earnings balance, without going through formal legal bankruptcy proceedings.
questions.explanationJa: 準再編成（quasi-reorganization）は、累積赤字（利益剰余金のマイナス残高）を解消するための会計上の手続きである。資産を公正価値に評価替えし、払込資本を縮小させて赤字を相殺することで、正式な法的倒産手続きなしに「フレッシュスタート」を与える。

questions.stem: The stockholders' equity section of Brown Co.'s December 31, Year 4, balance sheet consisted of the following: Common stock, $30 par, 10,000 shares authorized and outstanding $300,000 Additional paid-in capital 150,000 Retained earnings (deficit) (210,000) On January 2, Year 5, Brown put into effect a stockholder-approved quasi-reorganization by reducing the par value of the stock to $5 and eliminating the deficit against additional paid-in capital. Immediately after the quasi-reorganization, what amount should Brown report as additional paid-in capital?
